sources.full_text: Dwelling, Multifamily Stacked: A vertical and horizontal grouping of dwelling units, where at least 1 dwelling unit within the grouping contains 2 or more stories and is situated over or under another dwelling unit. Each unit has its own ground floor external entrance or shares its entrance with only an adjacent unit. Stacked multifamily dwelling units are commonly referred to as stacked townhomes, one over twos, and two over twos.

sources.title: Dwelling, Quadruplex
sources.excerpt: Dwelling, Quadruplex: A type of single-family attached dwelling that is 1 of a group of 4 contiguous single-family attached dwelling units within a building, arranged or designed in a manner that does not meet the definition of townhouse, attached to at least 1 of the other quadruplex dwelling units in the building, with each dwelling unit being located on a separate lot and separated from other dwelling units by 1 or more walls with no openings that serve as a generally vertical boundary betwee
sources.full_text: Dwelling, Quadruplex: A type of single-family attached dwelling that is 1 of a group of 4 contiguous single-family attached dwelling units within a building, arranged or designed in a manner that does not meet the definition of townhouse, attached to at least 1 of the other quadruplex dwelling units in the building, with each dwelling unit being located on a separate lot and separated from other dwelling units by 1 or more walls with no openings that serve as a generally vertical boundary between the dwelling units, and with each such dwelling unit extending from ground to roof, with no dwelling unit directly above another dwelling unit. Each quadruplex dwelling unit may consist of multiple floors or levels, with each unit having its own ground floor external entrance.

sources.title: Dwelling, Single-Family Attached
sources.excerpt: Dwelling, Single-Family Attached: A duplex, triplex, quadruplex, or townhouse dwelling unit.
sources.full_text: Dwelling, Single-Family Attached: A duplex, triplex, quadruplex, or townhouse dwelling unit.

sources.title: Dwelling, Single-Family Detached
sources.excerpt: Dwelling, Single-Family Detached: A dwelling unit designed for and occupied by 1 family only and not structurally connected or attached to any other dwelling and with each building having a separate lot, with minimum dimensions required by district regulations.
sources.full_text: Dwelling, Single-Family Detached: A dwelling unit designed for and occupied by 1 family only and not structurally connected or attached to any other dwelling and with each building having a separate lot, with minimum dimensions required by district regulations.

sources.title: Dwelling, Temporary
sources.excerpt: Dwelling, Temporary: A structure that is installed temporarily for use as a dwelling while a residence is being constructed. Such structure must be removed within 1 month of the completion of construction on the primary residence.
sources.full_text: Dwelling, Temporary: A structure that is installed temporarily for use as a dwelling while a residence is being constructed. Such structure must be removed within 1 month of the completion of construction on the primary residence.

sources.title: Dwelling, Tenant
sources.excerpt: Dwelling, Tenant: A dwelling located on the same property as an active agricultural, horticultural, or animal husbandry operation, occupied by a person or persons other than the owner of the lot on which it is located, who during their occupancy, is employed by and derives income from labor performed on the active agricultural, horticultural, or animal husbandry operation.
sources.full_text: Dwelling, Tenant: A dwelling located on the same property as an active agricultural, horticultural, or animal husbandry operation, occupied by a person or persons other than the owner of the lot on which it is located, who during their occupancy, is employed by and derives income from labor performed on the active agricultural, horticultural, or animal husbandry operation.

sources.title: Dwelling, Townhouse
sources.excerpt: Dwelling, Townhouse: A type of single-family attached dwelling that is 1 of a group of 3 to 8 contiguous and linearly arranged single-family attached dwelling units within a building, each of which abuts at least 1 of the other townhouse dwelling units in the building, with each dwelling unit being separated from other dwelling units by a wall with no openings that serves as a generally vertical boundary between the dwelling units, with each such dwelling unit extending from ground to roof, with
sources.full_text: Dwelling, Townhouse: A type of single-family attached dwelling that is 1 of a group of 3 to 8 contiguous and linearly arranged single-family attached dwelling units within a building, each of which abuts at least 1 of the other townhouse dwelling units in the building, with each dwelling unit being separated from other dwelling units by a wall with no openings that serves as a generally vertical boundary between the dwelling units, with each such dwelling unit extending from ground to roof, with no dwelling unit directly above another dwelling unit. Each townhouse dwelling unit may consist of multiple floors or levels, with each unit having its own ground floor external entrance or sharing its entrance with only an adjacent unit.

sources.title: Dwelling, Triplex
sources.excerpt: Dwelling, Triplex: A type of single-family attached dwelling that is 1 of a group of 3 contiguous dwelling units within a building, arranged or designed in a manner that does not meet the definition of townhouse, attached to at least 1 of the other triplex dwelling units in the building, with each dwelling unit being located on a separate lot and separated from other dwelling units by 1 or more walls with no openings that serve as a generally vertical boundary between the dwelling units, with ea
sources.full_text: Dwelling, Triplex: A type of single-family attached dwelling that is 1 of a group of 3 contiguous dwelling units within a building, arranged or designed in a manner that does not meet the definition of townhouse, attached to at least 1 of the other triplex dwelling units in the building, with each dwelling unit being located on a separate lot and separated from other dwelling units by 1 or more walls with no openings that serve as a generally vertical boundary between the dwelling units, with each such dwelling unit extending from ground to roof, with no dwelling unit directly above another dwelling unit. Each triplex dwelling unit may consist of multiple floors or levels, with each unit having its own ground floor external entrance.

sources.title: Dwelling Unit
sources.excerpt: Dwelling Unit: One room or rooms connected together, constituting a separate unit for a single-family owner occupancy or rental or lease, and physically separated from any other rooms or dwelling units that may be in the same structure, and containing independent cooking, sanitation, and sleeping facilities.
sources.full_text: Dwelling Unit: One room or rooms connected together, constituting a separate unit for a single-family owner occupancy or rental or lease, and physically separated from any other rooms or dwelling units that may be in the same structure, and containing independent cooking, sanitation, and sleeping facilities.

sources.title: Easement
sources.excerpt: Easement: A grant of interest in real property by the owner to, or for the use by, an abutting landowner, the public, or another person or entity, including both easements appurtenant and easements in gross.
sources.full_text: Easement: A grant of interest in real property by the owner to, or for the use by, an abutting landowner, the public, or another person or entity, including both easements appurtenant and easements in gross.

sources.title: Ecological Integrity
sources.excerpt: Ecological Integrity: Maintenance of the structure and functional attributes characteristic of a particular locale, or ecosystem, including normal variability.
sources.full_text: Ecological Integrity: Maintenance of the structure and functional attributes characteristic of a particular locale, or ecosystem, including normal variability.

sources.title: Ecosystem
sources.excerpt: Ecosystem: A complex network of organic communities and their interaction with their environment.
sources.full_text: Ecosystem: A complex network of organic communities and their interaction with their environment.

sources.title: Eco-tourism
sources.excerpt: Eco-tourism: See Cultural Tourism.
sources.full_text: Eco-tourism: See Cultural Tourism.

sources.title: Education
sources.excerpt: Education: Facilities for the education of students, including public and private schools at the primary, elementary, middle, or high school level, vocational and technical schools. Accessory uses include play areas, cafeterias, recreational and sport facilities, auditoriums, and before- or after-school day care.
sources.full_text: Education: Facilities for the education of students, including public and private schools at the primary, elementary, middle, or high school level, vocational and technical schools. Accessory uses include play areas, cafeterias, recreational and sport facilities, auditoriums, and before- or after-school day care.

sources.title: Educational or Research Facilities Use Related to the Agriculture, Horticulture, and Animal Husbandry Uses in the District
sources.excerpt: Educational or Research Facilities Use Related to the Agriculture, Horticulture, and Animal Husbandry Uses in the District: A facility for the investigation, testing, or demonstration of, or for training or educating persons in, products and processes related to agriculture, horticulture, or animal husbandry, including biotechnical agriculture, veterinary, soil, plant, and animal sciences.
sources.full_text: Educational or Research Facilities Use Related to the Agriculture, Horticulture, and Animal Husbandry Uses in the District: A facility for the investigation, testing, or demonstration of, or for training or educating persons in, products and processes related to agriculture, horticulture, or animal husbandry, including biotechnical agriculture, veterinary, soil, plant, and animal sciences.
sources.section_ref: Educational or Research Facilities Use Related to the Agriculture, Horticulture,

sources.title: Electric Generating Plant and Transmission Facility/Utility Generating Plant or Transmission Facility
sources.excerpt: Electric Generating Plant and Transmission Facility/Utility Generating Plant or Transmission Facility: A plant for the production of electricity, including appurtenant yards, equipment, and facilities for the storage of fuels, water, utility lines, exhaust stacks, and/or utility substation. This use does not include solar facilities.
sources.full_text: Electric Generating Plant and Transmission Facility/Utility Generating Plant or Transmission Facility: A plant for the production of electricity, including appurtenant yards, equipment, and facilities for the storage of fuels, water, utility lines, exhaust stacks, and/or utility substation. This use does not include solar facilities.
sources.section_ref: Electric Generating Plant and Transmission Facility/Utility Generating Plant or

sources.title: Electric Vehicle Charging Space
sources.excerpt: Electric Vehicle Charging Space: An automobile parking space that includes an electrical component assembly or cluster of component assemblies (battery charging station) designed and intended to transfer Level 2 charging (as defined by the United States Department of Energy) electric energy by conductive or inductive means from the electric grid or other off-board electrical source to a battery or other energy storage device within a vehicle that operates, partially or exclusively, on electric e
sources.full_text: Electric Vehicle Charging Space: An automobile parking space that includes an electrical component assembly or cluster of component assemblies (battery charging station) designed and intended to transfer Level 2 charging (as defined by the United States Department of Energy) electric energy by conductive or inductive means from the electric grid or other off-board electrical source to a battery or other energy storage device within a vehicle that operates, partially or exclusively, on electric energy, and is marked to indicate that such spaces are reserved for the sole use by plug-in electric vehicles.

sources.title: Electric Vehicle Supply Equipment (EVSE)
sources.excerpt: Electric Vehicle Supply Equipment (EVSE): Devices that provide electric power to a vehicle in order to recharge the vehicle's batteries. EVSE systems include the electrical conductors, related equipment, software, and communications protocols that deliver energy to the vehicle.
sources.full_text: Electric Vehicle Supply Equipment (EVSE): Devices that provide electric power to a vehicle in order to recharge the vehicle's batteries. EVSE systems include the electrical conductors, related equipment, software, and communications protocols that deliver energy to the vehicle.

sources.title: Emergency
sources.excerpt: Emergency: Any occurrence or set of circumstances involving actual or imminent physical trauma, property damage, or loss of electric or phone services that requires immediate action.
sources.full_text: Emergency: Any occurrence or set of circumstances involving actual or imminent physical trauma, property damage, or loss of electric or phone services that requires immediate action.

sources.title: Energy Storage Facility
sources.excerpt: Energy Storage Facility: Energy storage equipment or technology that is capable of absorbing energy, storing such energy for a period of time, and redelivering energy after it has been stored. A. Energy Storage, Utility Scale: One or more devices, assembled together, capable of storing energy in order to supply electrical energy at a future time, greater than 600kWh in nameplate capacity. B. Energy Storage, Site-Specific: One or more devices, assembled together, capable of storing energy in orde
sources.full_text: Energy Storage Facility: Energy storage equipment or technology that is capable of absorbing energy, storing such energy for a period of time, and redelivering energy after it has been stored. A. Energy Storage, Utility Scale: One or more devices, assembled together, capable of storing energy in order to supply electrical energy at a future time, greater than 600kWh in nameplate capacity. B. Energy Storage, Site-Specific: One or more devices, assembled together, capable of storing energy in order to supply electrical energy at a future time, less than or equal to 600kWh in nameplate capacity. Storage devices are an accessory use located on the property providing the energy generation source. This does not include energy storage facilities or devices associated with Solar Facility, Utility Scale.

sources.title: Enfront
sources.excerpt: Enfront: To face or to be opposite across a street.
sources.full_text: Enfront: To face or to be opposite across a street.

sources.title: Entertainment Facility
sources.excerpt: Entertainment Facility: An establishment where the primary source of revenue is derived from live or recorded performances shown or played for the amusement of an audience. Examples include music clubs and dance halls.
sources.full_text: Entertainment Facility: An establishment where the primary source of revenue is derived from live or recorded performances shown or played for the amusement of an audience. Examples include music clubs and dance halls.

sources.title: Equestrian Event Facility
sources.excerpt: Equestrian Event Facility: An establishment engaged in equine activities and events including teaching equestrian skills, participating in equestrian competitions, exhibitions, or other displays of equestrian skill (such as polo, dressage, and show jumping). Accessory uses may include offices, storage areas, caretaker’s quarters, and caring for, breeding, boarding, riding, or training horses associated with the Equestrian Event Facility use.
sources.full_text: Equestrian Event Facility: An establishment engaged in equine activities and events including teaching equestrian skills, participating in equestrian competitions, exhibitions, or other displays of equestrian skill (such as polo, dressage, and show jumping). Accessory uses may include offices, storage areas, caretaker’s quarters, and caring for, breeding, boarding, riding, or training horses associated with the Equestrian Event Facility use.

sources.title: Erected
sources.excerpt: Erected: Constructed, reconstructed, moved, or structurally altered.
sources.full_text: Erected: Constructed, reconstructed, moved, or structurally altered.

sources.title: Establishment
sources.excerpt: Establishment: A business, institution, place, or entity that operates or hosts a use, including any business, trade, or occupation. This includes all buildings, structures, land, facilities, or equipment engaged in operating the use.
sources.full_text: Establishment: A business, institution, place, or entity that operates or hosts a use, including any business, trade, or occupation. This includes all buildings, structures, land, facilities, or equipment engaged in operating the use.

sources.title: Extractive Industries
sources.excerpt: Extractive Industries: The excavation, mining, dredging, or stripping of land or earth, including quarrying or borrow pits; or the extraction of groundwater (Water Extraction). "Quarrying" means commercial or industrial stone quarries or operations involving removal from a site of natural accumulations of sand, rock, soil, or gravel. "Stone quarrying" includes appurtenant structures such as crushers, screeners, and washers. Accessory uses include: A. Crushing, treating, washing, and/or processin
sources.full_text: Extractive Industries: The excavation, mining, dredging, or stripping of land or earth, including quarrying or borrow pits; or the extraction of groundwater (Water Extraction). "Quarrying" means commercial or industrial stone quarries or operations involving removal from a site of natural accumulations of sand, rock, soil, or gravel. "Stone quarrying" includes appurtenant structures such as crushers, screeners, and washers. Accessory uses include: A. Crushing, treating, washing, and/or processing of materials, accessory to a quarry operation, when conducted on the same property; B. Manufacturing of concrete block, cinderblock, or pre-formed concrete products, accessory to an approved quarry use; C. Retail sales of crushed stone or architectural stone products, accessory to an approved quarry use; and D. Concrete batching plants or asphalt mixing plants consistent with Section 4.06.03 (Extractive Industries).

sources.title: Façade
sources.excerpt: Façade: The exterior wall of a building exposed to public view or a wall viewed by persons not within the building.
sources.full_text: Façade: The exterior wall of a building exposed to public view or a wall viewed by persons not within the building.

sources.title: Facilities Standards Manual
sources.excerpt: Facilities Standards Manual: The Loudoun County Facilities Standards Manual.
sources.full_text: Facilities Standards Manual: The Loudoun County Facilities Standards Manual.

sources.title: Facility
sources.excerpt: Facility: A building, structure, place, amenity, or piece of equipment that is provided or reserved for a particular use or purpose.
sources.full_text: Facility: A building, structure, place, amenity, or piece of equipment that is provided or reserved for a particular use or purpose.

sources.title: Family
sources.excerpt: Family: A group of people living together consisting of: A. One or more persons related by blood or marriage together with any number of natural, foster, step, or adopted children, domestic servants, nurses, and therapists and no more than 2 roomers or boarders; or B. No more than 4 persons, not related by blood or marriage; or C. Any group identified in Code of Virginia § 15.2-2291.
sources.full_text: Family: A group of people living together consisting of: A. One or more persons related by blood or marriage together with any number of natural, foster, step, or adopted children, domestic servants, nurses, and therapists and no more than 2 roomers or boarders; or B. No more than 4 persons, not related by blood or marriage; or C. Any group identified in Code of Virginia § 15.2-2291.

sources.title: Farm-based Tourism
sources.excerpt: Farm-based Tourism: See Cultural Tourism.
sources.full_text: Farm-based Tourism: See Cultural Tourism.

sources.title: Farm Co-Op
sources.excerpt: Farm Co-Op: A facility used by an organization of farm producers for co-operative technical and marketing assistance. A farm co-op may include a central marketplace where farmers can deliver products for pick-up by consumers but is not a wholesale distribution center.
sources.full_text: Farm Co-Op: A facility used by an organization of farm producers for co-operative technical and marketing assistance. A farm co-op may include a central marketplace where farmers can deliver products for pick-up by consumers but is not a wholesale distribution center.

sources.title: Farm Distribution Hub
sources.excerpt: Farm Distribution Hub: A place where farmers can deliver agricultural products for pick-up by consumers or wholesalers, but not including a central place operated by a farm co-op where farmers can deliver products for pick-up by consumers (see Farm Co-op). Farm distribution hub does not include such uses as trucking operations, stockyards, auction houses, slaughterhouses, canneries, or other processing facilities.
sources.full_text: Farm Distribution Hub: A place where farmers can deliver agricultural products for pick-up by consumers or wholesalers, but not including a central place operated by a farm co-op where farmers can deliver products for pick-up by consumers (see Farm Co-op). Farm distribution hub does not include such uses as trucking operations, stockyards, auction houses, slaughterhouses, canneries, or other processing facilities.

sources.title: Farm Machinery Sales and Service
sources.excerpt: Farm Machinery Sales and Service: An establishment for the sale, rental, and/or service of equipment normally or routinely used on farms and gardens, and related parts, tools, and accessories, but not of non-farm equipment or materials.
sources.full_text: Farm Machinery Sales and Service: An establishment for the sale, rental, and/or service of equipment normally or routinely used on farms and gardens, and related parts, tools, and accessories, but not of non-farm equipment or materials.

sources.title: Farmers Market
sources.excerpt: Farmers Market: A principal use that includes the sale of unprocessed and/or processed aquacultural, horticultural, and/or agricultural products. Examples include nursery stock, perennials, annuals, bulbs, mulch, compost, dried flowers, Christmas trees and greens, fresh produce, honey, cider, and similar aquacultural, horticultual, or agricultural products. A. Farmers Market, On-Site Production: A use that includes the sale of products that are grown and processed on-site. B. Farmers Market, Off
sources.full_text: Farmers Market: A principal use that includes the sale of unprocessed and/or processed aquacultural, horticultural, and/or agricultural products. Examples include nursery stock, perennials, annuals, bulbs, mulch, compost, dried flowers, Christmas trees and greens, fresh produce, honey, cider, and similar aquacultural, horticultual, or agricultural products. A. Farmers Market, On-Site Production: A use that includes the sale of products that are grown and processed on-site. B. Farmers Market, Off-Site Production: A use that includes the sale of products that are grown and/or processed off-site. The use may or may not be located on the site of ongoing aquacultural, horticultural, or agricultural activity. This use may include the sale of products from 1 or more vendors. C. Farmers Market, Temporary: A temporary use in which stands or sales areas are set aside and rented or otherwise provided that includes the sale of products that are grown and/or processed on-site or off-site and is permitted for no more than 3 days per week per location. A Temporary Farmers Market does not include permanent structures and requires approval of a temporary Zoning Permit by the Zoning Administrator subject to Section 10.04.C.3. A Temporary Farmers Market may include temporary retail and the sale of products from 1 or more vendors.

sources.title: Fee Simple Ownership
sources.excerpt: Fee Simple Ownership: The complete ownership interest in real property; the ownership of the entire “bundle” of rights attached to real property.
sources.full_text: Fee Simple Ownership: The complete ownership interest in real property; the ownership of the entire “bundle” of rights attached to real property.

sources.title: Feed and Farm Supply Center
sources.excerpt: Feed and Farm Supply Center: A commercial establishment engaged in the provision of animal feed, bedding and accessories, and farm and garden supplies (such as seed, fencing, hardware, pesticides, and fertilizer) to agricultural, horticultural, and/or animal husbandry operations. Also known as a “feed-and-seed” store.
sources.full_text: Feed and Farm Supply Center: A commercial establishment engaged in the provision of animal feed, bedding and accessories, and farm and garden supplies (such as seed, fencing, hardware, pesticides, and fertilizer) to agricultural, horticultural, and/or animal husbandry operations. Also known as a “feed-and-seed” store.

sources.title: Feedlot
sources.excerpt: Feedlot: An enclosure (including a lot, yard, building, or corral) in which animals fed for slaughter are confined, that is used for more than 30 days in a 1-year period, with or without an area for the raising of crops, forage, or other vegetation and where animals fed for slaughter are allowed to graze or feed. A feedlot does not include any area where animals are held for slaughter by a processor for a period of 30 days or less.
sources.full_text: Feedlot: An enclosure (including a lot, yard, building, or corral) in which animals fed for slaughter are confined, that is used for more than 30 days in a 1-year period, with or without an area for the raising of crops, forage, or other vegetation and where animals fed for slaughter are allowed to graze or feed. A feedlot does not include any area where animals are held for slaughter by a processor for a period of 30 days or less.

sources.title: Fenestration
sources.excerpt: Fenestration: An architectural feature that provides a real, functional opening that allows light to pass or filter through; or faux opening (a decorative veneer giving the appearance of an opening) in the building façade, through the installation of curtain walls or recessed or protruding windows or doors.
sources.full_text: Fenestration: An architectural feature that provides a real, functional opening that allows light to pass or filter through; or faux opening (a decorative veneer giving the appearance of an opening) in the building façade, through the installation of curtain walls or recessed or protruding windows or doors.

sources.title: Fire and/or Rescue Station
sources.excerpt: Fire and/or Rescue Station: Facilities for the provision of local rapid response emergency services such as firefighting and mobile medical emergency services, including areas for the storage and maintenance of emergency vehicles and equipment and housing and feeding of emergency personnel.
sources.full_text: Fire and/or Rescue Station: Facilities for the provision of local rapid response emergency services such as firefighting and mobile medical emergency services, including areas for the storage and maintenance of emergency vehicles and equipment and housing and feeding of emergency personnel.

sources.title: Fiscal
sources.excerpt: Fiscal: Of or relating to public revenues, public expenditures, and public debt; public financial matters.
sources.full_text: Fiscal: Of or relating to public revenues, public expenditures, and public debt; public financial matters.

sources.title: Flex Building
sources.excerpt: Flex Building: A building designed to accommodate a combination of uses, the exact proportions of each use being subject to user needs over time and the applicable zoning district.
sources.full_text: Flex Building: A building designed to accommodate a combination of uses, the exact proportions of each use being subject to user needs over time and the applicable zoning district.

sources.title: Floor Area, Gross
sources.excerpt: Floor Area, Gross: The sum of the total horizontal areas of the several floors of all buildings on a lot, measured from the interior faces of exterior walls. When calculating Gross Floor Area, include: A. Basements; B. Elevator shafts and stairwells at each story; C. Floor space used for mechanical equipment with structural headroom of 6 feet, 6 inches or more; D. Penthouses; E. Attic space, whether or not a floor has actually been laid, providing structural headroom of 6 feet, 6 inches or more;
sources.full_text: Floor Area, Gross: The sum of the total horizontal areas of the several floors of all buildings on a lot, measured from the interior faces of exterior walls. When calculating Gross Floor Area, include: A. Basements; B. Elevator shafts and stairwells at each story; C. Floor space used for mechanical equipment with structural headroom of 6 feet, 6 inches or more; D. Penthouses; E. Attic space, whether or not a floor has actually been laid, providing structural headroom of 6 feet, 6 inches or more; and F. Interior balconies and mezzanines. Gross floor area does not include: A. Cellars or outside balconies that do not exceed a projection of 6 feet beyond the exterior walls of the building; and B. Parking structures below or above grade and rooftop mechanical structures.

sources.title: Floor Area Ratio (FAR)
sources.excerpt: Floor Area Ratio (FAR): A number or percentage, derived by dividing the gross floor area of the buildings on any lot by the lot area in square feet. The floor area ratio multiplied by the lot area in square feet produces the maximum amount of floor area that may be constructed on such lot.
sources.full_text: Floor Area Ratio (FAR): A number or percentage, derived by dividing the gross floor area of the buildings on any lot by the lot area in square feet. The floor area ratio multiplied by the lot area in square feet produces the maximum amount of floor area that may be constructed on such lot.

sources.title: Focal Point
sources.excerpt: Focal Point: An identifiable space centered in a community and conveniently accessed by its residents that serves as a place for gathering and communicating neighborhood identity, such as a central park, plaza, or green (including village green and town green).
sources.full_text: Focal Point: An identifiable space centered in a community and conveniently accessed by its residents that serves as a place for gathering and communicating neighborhood identity, such as a central park, plaza, or green (including village green and town green).

sources.title: Food and Beverage Sales/Service
sources.excerpt: Food and Beverage Sales/Service: A use category composed of facilities for serving prepared food or beverages for consumption on the premises, other than in a private residence or accessory to a principal use.
sources.full_text: Food and Beverage Sales/Service: A use category composed of facilities for serving prepared food or beverages for consumption on the premises, other than in a private residence or accessory to a principal use.

sources.title: Food Preparation
sources.excerpt: Food Preparation: An establishment for small-scale production of food and beverages for off-site consumption, including delivery services and catering services. This classification excludes food production of an industrial character (See Manufacturing, General).
sources.full_text: Food Preparation: An establishment for small-scale production of food and beverages for off-site consumption, including delivery services and catering services. This classification excludes food production of an industrial character (See Manufacturing, General).

sources.title: Foot Candles
sources.excerpt: Foot Candles: A unit of illumination; technically, the illumination at all points 1 foot distant from a uniform point source of 1 candle power.
sources.full_text: Foot Candles: A unit of illumination; technically, the illumination at all points 1 foot distant from a uniform point source of 1 candle power.

sources.title: Forestry
sources.excerpt: Forestry: The planting, growing and harvesting of trees, but not including sawmilling or other processing of trees or parts thereof.
sources.full_text: Forestry: The planting, growing and harvesting of trees, but not including sawmilling or other processing of trees or parts thereof.

sources.title: Frontage
sources.excerpt: Frontage: The portion of a lot that abuts a road.
sources.full_text: Frontage: The portion of a lot that abuts a road.

sources.title: Functionally or Economically Obsolete
sources.excerpt: Functionally or Economically Obsolete: The use for which a structure was originally constructed is no longer viable, necessary, or productive, or the structure for which the original use was constructed is functionally inadequate, causing the structure to no longer be used for the original intent, have a high rate of vacancy, and/or be in a state of disrepair. Examples could include historic schools, churches, agricultural buildings, and fraternal organization meeting halls.
sources.full_text: Functionally or Economically Obsolete: The use for which a structure was originally constructed is no longer viable, necessary, or productive, or the structure for which the original use was constructed is functionally inadequate, causing the structure to no longer be used for the original intent, have a high rate of vacancy, and/or be in a state of disrepair. Examples could include historic schools, churches, agricultural buildings, and fraternal organization meeting halls.

sources.title: Funeral Home
sources.excerpt: Funeral Home: An establishment used primarily for human funeral services and may or may not include facilities on the premises for embalming and performing autopsies or other surgical procedures.
sources.full_text: Funeral Home: An establishment used primarily for human funeral services and may or may not include facilities on the premises for embalming and performing autopsies or other surgical procedures.

sources.title: Garden
sources.excerpt: Garden: A plot of cultivated ground adjacent to a dwelling, devoted in whole or in part to the growing of herbs, fruits, flowers, or vegetables for consumption by the household residing in the dwelling.
sources.full_text: Garden: A plot of cultivated ground adjacent to a dwelling, devoted in whole or in part to the growing of herbs, fruits, flowers, or vegetables for consumption by the household residing in the dwelling.

sources.title: Garden, Native Plant
sources.excerpt: Garden, Native Plant: A planted type of Public Garden where the majority of the plants are native plants.
sources.full_text: Garden, Native Plant: A planted type of Public Garden where the majority of the plants are native plants.

sources.title: Garden, Public
sources.excerpt: Garden, Public: A publicly accessible planted area that may also include paths, seating areas, and other hardscape features, and is maintained for the primary purpose of public gathering space, public passive recreation, and/or public environmental education, research, or conservation.
sources.full_text: Garden, Public: A publicly accessible planted area that may also include paths, seating areas, and other hardscape features, and is maintained for the primary purpose of public gathering space, public passive recreation, and/or public environmental education, research, or conservation.

sources.title: Geotechnical Report
sources.excerpt: Geotechnical Report: A study of bedrock and soils used to help determine potential safe development that is prepared according to standards in the Loudoun County Facilities Standards Manual.
sources.full_text: Geotechnical Report: A study of bedrock and soils used to help determine potential safe development that is prepared according to standards in the Loudoun County Facilities Standards Manual.

sources.title: Golf Driving Ranges
sources.excerpt: Golf Driving Ranges: A limited area on which golf players do not walk, but onto which they drive golf balls from central driving tees. A golf driving range may contain outdoor lighting. A golf driving range may have, as an accessory use, a putting or chipping green, miniature golf course, baseball batting cages, an accessory snack bar or refreshment stand, and/or equipment rental of items pertaining to golf and baseball.
sources.full_text: Golf Driving Ranges: A limited area on which golf players do not walk, but onto which they drive golf balls from central driving tees. A golf driving range may contain outdoor lighting. A golf driving range may have, as an accessory use, a putting or chipping green, miniature golf course, baseball batting cages, an accessory snack bar or refreshment stand, and/or equipment rental of items pertaining to golf and baseball.

sources.title: Government (General)
sources.excerpt: Government (General): Any area, building, or structure held, used, or controlled exclusively for public purposes by any department or branch of the Federal Government, Commonwealth of Virginia, or the Loudoun County government. For purposes of the Use Tables, "government (general)" includes only uses defined above that are not otherwise listed in the Use Tables.
sources.full_text: Government (General): Any area, building, or structure held, used, or controlled exclusively for public purposes by any department or branch of the Federal Government, Commonwealth of Virginia, or the Loudoun County government. For purposes of the Use Tables, "government (general)" includes only uses defined above that are not otherwise listed in the Use Tables.

sources.title: Green
sources.excerpt: Green: A non-paved public open space within the Town Center Core of a TC Zoning District, a residential development, or other mixed-use development containing a well-defined green space with landscaped areas. A green is a type of Community Open Space.
sources.full_text: Green: A non-paved public open space within the Town Center Core of a TC Zoning District, a residential development, or other mixed-use development containing a well-defined green space with landscaped areas. A green is a type of Community Open Space.

sources.title: Green Roof
sources.excerpt: Green Roof: A Green Roof is either of the following: A. Solar Roof: A solar roofing system that generates reusable energy, and such reusable energy accounts for at least 2.5% of the total electric energy used by the building to which the solar roofing system is attached; or B. Vegetative Roof: A roofing system designed in accordance with the Virginia Stormwater Management Program's standards and specifications for green roofs, as set forth in the Virginia Stormwater BMP Clearinghouse, in which a
sources.full_text: Green Roof: A Green Roof is either of the following: A. Solar Roof: A solar roofing system that generates reusable energy, and such reusable energy accounts for at least 2.5% of the total electric energy used by the building to which the solar roofing system is attached; or B. Vegetative Roof: A roofing system designed in accordance with the Virginia Stormwater Management Program's standards and specifications for green roofs, as set forth in the Virginia Stormwater BMP Clearinghouse, in which at least 50% of the total roofing area is vegetative.

sources.title: Green-Wall
sources.excerpt: Green-Wall: A vertically constructed element of a structure or building that is functionally designed with a primary purpose of accommodating or featuring living plants across the surface area of the subject structure or building plane.
sources.full_text: Green-Wall: A vertically constructed element of a structure or building that is functionally designed with a primary purpose of accommodating or featuring living plants across the surface area of the subject structure or building plane.

sources.title: Greenway
sources.excerpt: Greenway: A network of natural corridors that connect areas of open space. It follows natural land or water features, like ridges or rivers, canals, or human landscape features such as an abandoned railroad or historic sites.
sources.full_text: Greenway: A network of natural corridors that connect areas of open space. It follows natural land or water features, like ridges or rivers, canals, or human landscape features such as an abandoned railroad or historic sites.

sources.title: Ground Passenger Transportation (e.g. Taxi, Charter Bus)
sources.excerpt: Ground Passenger Transportation (e.g., Taxi, Charter Bus): Establishments that provide passenger transportation by bus, charter bus, automobile, limousine, van, or shuttle. Some services (such as taxi) are not operated over regular routes and on regular schedules. Examples include charter bus, special needs transportation, taxicab owner/operators, taxicab fleet operators, or taxicab organizations. This use includes fleet services that store, maintain, repair, fuel, and service 2 or more vehicles
sources.full_text: Ground Passenger Transportation (e.g., Taxi, Charter Bus): Establishments that provide passenger transportation by bus, charter bus, automobile, limousine, van, or shuttle. Some services (such as taxi) are not operated over regular routes and on regular schedules. Examples include charter bus, special needs transportation, taxicab owner/operators, taxicab fleet operators, or taxicab organizations. This use includes fleet services that store, maintain, repair, fuel, and service 2 or more vehicles owned by a single commercial or public entity. This use does not include publicly operated bus or transit systems.
sources.section_ref: Ground Passenger Transportation (e.g. Taxi, Charter Bus)

sources.title: Groundwater
sources.excerpt: Groundwater: Any water, except capillary moisture, beneath the land surface in the zone of saturation or beneath the bed of any stream, lake, reservoir or other body of surface water, whatever may be the subsurface geologic structure in which such water stands, flows, percolates, or otherwise occurs.
sources.full_text: Groundwater: Any water, except capillary moisture, beneath the land surface in the zone of saturation or beneath the bed of any stream, lake, reservoir or other body of surface water, whatever may be the subsurface geologic structure in which such water stands, flows, percolates, or otherwise occurs.

sources.title: Group Living
sources.excerpt: Group Living: Uses characterized by the residential occupancy of a structure by a group of persons that does not meet the definition of Household Living. Tenancy is arranged on a weekly or longer basis, and the size of the group may be larger than a single family. Generally, Group Living structures have a common eating area for residents. The residents may receive care, training, or treatment at the place of residence, and caregivers may or may not also reside at the site. Accessory uses commonl
sources.full_text: Group Living: Uses characterized by the residential occupancy of a structure by a group of persons that does not meet the definition of Household Living. Tenancy is arranged on a weekly or longer basis, and the size of the group may be larger than a single family. Generally, Group Living structures have a common eating area for residents. The residents may receive care, training, or treatment at the place of residence, and caregivers may or may not also reside at the site. Accessory uses commonly associated with Group Living are recreational facilities and vehicle parking for occupants and staff.

sources.title: Guest Room
sources.excerpt: Guest Room: A habitable room used or intended to be used for sleeping purposes only on a transient basis. A guest room may have its own or a common bathroom and is not permitted to have independent kitchen facilities.
sources.full_text: Guest Room: A habitable room used or intended to be used for sleeping purposes only on a transient basis. A guest room may have its own or a common bathroom and is not permitted to have independent kitchen facilities.

sources.title: Habitat
sources.excerpt: Habitat: The place or environment where animals or plants naturally or normally live and grow.
sources.full_text: Habitat: The place or environment where animals or plants naturally or normally live and grow.

sources.title: Hazardous Substance
sources.excerpt: Hazardous Substance: Any material that poses a threat to human health and/or the environment. Typical hazardous substances are toxic, corrosive, ignitable, explosive, or chemically reactive. Such substances must include any substance designated by the United States Environmental Protection Agency to be reported if a designated quantity of the substance is spilled in the waters of the United States or is otherwise released into the environment.
sources.full_text: Hazardous Substance: Any material that poses a threat to human health and/or the environment. Typical hazardous substances are toxic, corrosive, ignitable, explosive, or chemically reactive. Such substances must include any substance designated by the United States Environmental Protection Agency to be reported if a designated quantity of the substance is spilled in the waters of the United States or is otherwise released into the environment.

sources.title: Headwaters
sources.excerpt: Headwaters: The source of a stream or river.
sources.full_text: Headwaters: The source of a stream or river.

sources.title: Health and Fitness Center
sources.excerpt: Health and Fitness Center: An establishment offering or providing facilities for, and instruction in, general health, physical fitness, and controlled exercises including weightlifting, calisthenics, and aerobics. A Health and Fitness Center may include a spa or sauna as an accessory use.
sources.full_text: Health and Fitness Center: An establishment offering or providing facilities for, and instruction in, general health, physical fitness, and controlled exercises including weightlifting, calisthenics, and aerobics. A Health and Fitness Center may include a spa or sauna as an accessory use.

sources.title: Health Care
sources.excerpt: Health Care: Facilities principally engaged in providing services for health maintenance and treatment of mental or physical conditions. Accessory uses may include offices, meeting areas, on-site eating facilities for employees, laboratories, parking, and maintenance facilities.
sources.full_text: Health Care: Facilities principally engaged in providing services for health maintenance and treatment of mental or physical conditions. Accessory uses may include offices, meeting areas, on-site eating facilities for employees, laboratories, parking, and maintenance facilities.

sources.title: Health Official
sources.excerpt: Health Official: The Director of the Loudoun County Department of Health, or his designated deputy; Health Officer.
sources.full_text: Health Official: The Director of the Loudoun County Department of Health, or his designated deputy; Health Officer.

sources.title: Heavy Equipment
sources.excerpt: Heavy Equipment: Bulldozers, dump trucks, and similar pieces of large equipment and their accessories. The term includes any vehicle associated with a business not included in the definition of business vehicle. A trailer used for transporting heavy equipment is considered accessory to the equipment.
sources.full_text: Heavy Equipment: Bulldozers, dump trucks, and similar pieces of large equipment and their accessories. The term includes any vehicle associated with a business not included in the definition of business vehicle. A trailer used for transporting heavy equipment is considered accessory to the equipment.

sources.title: Heavy Equipment and Specialty Vehicle Sales and Accessory Service
sources.excerpt: Heavy Equipment and Specialty Vehicle Sales and Accessory Service: Buildings and premises for the sale, rental, and servicing of trucks, buses, boats, recreational vehicles, taxicabs, ambulances, mobile homes, trailers, and farm and construction machinery or equipment. This category must not be deemed to include automobile sales and accessory service establishment.
sources.full_text: Heavy Equipment and Specialty Vehicle Sales and Accessory Service: Buildings and premises for the sale, rental, and servicing of trucks, buses, boats, recreational vehicles, taxicabs, ambulances, mobile homes, trailers, and farm and construction machinery or equipment. This category must not be deemed to include automobile sales and accessory service establishment.
sources.section_ref: Heavy Equipment and Specialty Vehicle Sales and Accessory Service

sources.title: Heliport or Helistop
sources.excerpt: Heliport or Helistop: An area, either at ground level or elevated on a structure, licensed or approved for the landing and takeoff of helicopters and that may include accessory parking, waiting room, fueling, and maintenance equipment (heliport). This includes an area designed to accommodate touch-down and lift-off of helicopters to pick up and discharge passengers or cargo. This use does not include operation facilities other than 1 tie-down space and additional facilities required by law, ordi
sources.full_text: Heliport or Helistop: An area, either at ground level or elevated on a structure, licensed or approved for the landing and takeoff of helicopters and that may include accessory parking, waiting room, fueling, and maintenance equipment (heliport). This includes an area designed to accommodate touch-down and lift-off of helicopters to pick up and discharge passengers or cargo. This use does not include operation facilities other than 1 tie-down space and additional facilities required by law, ordinance, or regulation (helistop).

sources.title: Historic Resource
sources.excerpt: Historic Resource: A historic resource is a property or structure that has 1 of the following historic designations: A. Designated as or a contributing resource in a National Historic Landmark; B. Listed or eligible for listing in the National Register of Historic Places or a contributing resource in a historic district listed or eligible for listing in the National Register of Historic Places; C. Listed or eligible for listing in the Virginia Landmarks Register or a contributing resource in a h
sources.full_text: Historic Resource: A historic resource is a property or structure that has 1 of the following historic designations: A. Designated as or a contributing resource in a National Historic Landmark; B. Listed or eligible for listing in the National Register of Historic Places or a contributing resource in a historic district listed or eligible for listing in the National Register of Historic Places; C. Listed or eligible for listing in the Virginia Landmarks Register or a contributing resource in a historic district listed or eligible for listing in the Virginia Landmarks Register; D. Listed on the Loudoun County Heritage Register or a contributing resource in a historic district listed on the Loudoun County Heritage Register; or E. Designated as 1 of the following types of Historic Overlay Districts (HOD) pursuant to Section 10.10.08: 1. Loudoun County Historic Site (HS) District; 2. Contributing resource in a Loudoun County Historic and Cultural Conservation (HCC) District; or 3. Contributing resource in a Loudoun County Historic Roadways (HR) District.

sources.title: Historic Setting
sources.excerpt: Historic Setting: The exterior portion of a historic resource or property that contributes to the historic significance or character of the historic resource. Examples include walls, walkways, trees, gardens, relationship of a historic structure to the road, and relationship of a historic structure to landforms or topography.
sources.full_text: Historic Setting: The exterior portion of a historic resource or property that contributes to the historic significance or character of the historic resource. Examples include walls, walkways, trees, gardens, relationship of a historic structure to the road, and relationship of a historic structure to landforms or topography.

sources.title: Home Occupation
sources.excerpt: Home Occupation: A business, profession, occupation, or trade conducted for gain or support within a residential building or its accessory buildings. A Home Occupation use is incidental and secondary to the use of the buildings for dwelling purposes and does not change the residential character of such buildings. (See 3.03.E. for Home Occupation standards).
sources.full_text: Home Occupation: A business, profession, occupation, or trade conducted for gain or support within a residential building or its accessory buildings. A Home Occupation use is incidental and secondary to the use of the buildings for dwelling purposes and does not change the residential character of such buildings. (See 3.03.E. for Home Occupation standards).

sources.title: Horse Trails or Networks
sources.excerpt: Horse Trails or Networks: Paths or trails maintained for the purpose of horseback or other animal-mounted recreational riding, but not including racetracks.
sources.full_text: Horse Trails or Networks: Paths or trails maintained for the purpose of horseback or other animal-mounted recreational riding, but not including racetracks.

sources.title: Horticulture
sources.excerpt: Horticulture: The active and ongoing cultivation and production of orchard, garden, or nursery crops on a small or large scale, including the production of Christmas trees, field-grown crops, specialty crops, flowers, fruit, grapes (vineyard), market gardening, nursery stock, nuts, ornamental plants, sod, vegetables, and similar horticultural uses; and the cultivation of that produce by means of biotechnical or genetic engineering techniques. Horticulture does not include preparatory functions s
sources.full_text: Horticulture: The active and ongoing cultivation and production of orchard, garden, or nursery crops on a small or large scale, including the production of Christmas trees, field-grown crops, specialty crops, flowers, fruit, grapes (vineyard), market gardening, nursery stock, nuts, ornamental plants, sod, vegetables, and similar horticultural uses; and the cultivation of that produce by means of biotechnical or genetic engineering techniques. Horticulture does not include preparatory functions such as grading or creation of planting beds through stockpiling of dirt or other means when those preparations do not result in an active and ongoing horticultural activity within 30 days.

sources.title: Hospital
sources.excerpt: Hospital: Any licensed and Commonwealth of Virginia accredited health care institution with an organized medical and professional staff and with inpatient beds available around-the-clock whose primary function is to provide inpatient medical, nursing, and other health-related services to patients for both surgical and nonsurgical conditions and that usually provides some outpatient services, particularly emergency care.
sources.full_text: Hospital: Any licensed and Commonwealth of Virginia accredited health care institution with an organized medical and professional staff and with inpatient beds available around-the-clock whose primary function is to provide inpatient medical, nursing, and other health-related services to patients for both surgical and nonsurgical conditions and that usually provides some outpatient services, particularly emergency care.

sources.title: Hotel/Motel
sources.excerpt: Hotel/Motel: Any single building or group of buildings, combined or separated, containing 20 or more lodging units. This use may include features such as conference rooms, a restaurant, accessory snack bar or refreshment stand, or swimming pool or exercise room that would attract clientele other than transient guests.
sources.full_text: Hotel/Motel: Any single building or group of buildings, combined or separated, containing 20 or more lodging units. This use may include features such as conference rooms, a restaurant, accessory snack bar or refreshment stand, or swimming pool or exercise room that would attract clientele other than transient guests.

sources.title: Hours of Operation
sources.excerpt: Hours of Operation: The time period during which an activity or enterprise is active, including any times during which the activity is open to customers or other members of the public, employees are present and working, deliveries are made, or equipment (other than utilities or ordinary indoor appliances) is being actively operated on the site.
sources.full_text: Hours of Operation: The time period during which an activity or enterprise is active, including any times during which the activity is open to customers or other members of the public, employees are present and working, deliveries are made, or equipment (other than utilities or ordinary indoor appliances) is being actively operated on the site.

sources.title: Household Living
sources.excerpt: Household Living: Uses characterized by the residential occupancy of a dwelling unit by a family, whether owned by the occupant or otherwise where tenancy is arranged on a month-to-month or longer basis. Accessory uses may include recreational activities, caring for pets, gardens, personal storage structures, hobbies, and parking of occupants’ vehicles.
sources.full_text: Household Living: Uses characterized by the residential occupancy of a dwelling unit by a family, whether owned by the occupant or otherwise where tenancy is arranged on a month-to-month or longer basis. Accessory uses may include recreational activities, caring for pets, gardens, personal storage structures, hobbies, and parking of occupants’ vehicles.

sources.title: Hydrogeological Report
sources.excerpt: Hydrogeological Report: A study of groundwater, its location and prevalence that is prepared according to standards in the Facilities Standards Manual (FSM).
sources.full_text: Hydrogeological Report: A study of groundwater, its location and prevalence that is prepared according to standards in the Facilities Standards Manual (FSM).

sources.title: Illumination, External
sources.excerpt: Illumination, External: Illumination by a light source that is external to the object or structure that contains the illuminated surface.
sources.full_text: Illumination, External: Illumination by a light source that is external to the object or structure that contains the illuminated surface.

sources.title: Illumination, Halo Lit
sources.excerpt: Illumination, Halo Lit: Illumination created by concealing the light source behind three-dimensional opaque letters, numbers, or other characters of a sign, resulting in the nighttime perception of a halo around the silhouette of each character. This is also referred to as "reverse channel" or "reverse lit" illumination. A halo lit sign is not considered an internally illuminated sign.
sources.full_text: Illumination, Halo Lit: Illumination created by concealing the light source behind three-dimensional opaque letters, numbers, or other characters of a sign, resulting in the nighttime perception of a halo around the silhouette of each character. This is also referred to as "reverse channel" or "reverse lit" illumination. A halo lit sign is not considered an internally illuminated sign.

sources.title: Impermeable Surface
sources.excerpt: Impermeable Surface: Any material such as paved parking areas, sidewalks, or trail surfaces, that prevents absorption of storm water in or through such surface but must not include permeable or "pervious" paving materials.
sources.full_text: Impermeable Surface: Any material such as paved parking areas, sidewalks, or trail surfaces, that prevents absorption of storm water in or through such surface but must not include permeable or "pervious" paving materials.

sources.title: Impulsive Sound
sources.excerpt: Impulsive Sound: Sound of a short duration, usually less than 1 second, with an abrupt onset and rapid decline. Examples of sources of impulsive sound include, but are not limited to, punch press, discharge of firearm, and explosive blasting.
sources.full_text: Impulsive Sound: Sound of a short duration, usually less than 1 second, with an abrupt onset and rapid decline. Examples of sources of impulsive sound include, but are not limited to, punch press, discharge of firearm, and explosive blasting.

sources.title: Incremental Addition
sources.excerpt: Incremental Addition: An addition to an existing Zoning District made in conformance to the Zoning District's regulations and that is not required to meet the minimum zoning district size.
sources.full_text: Incremental Addition: An addition to an existing Zoning District made in conformance to the Zoning District's regulations and that is not required to meet the minimum zoning district size.

sources.title: Industrial Storage
sources.excerpt: Industrial Storage: A fixed installation where any of the following are stored, either for subsequent transshipment to a smaller fixed installation or for pick-up by truck for transport to the site where the product will be used by the consumer: A. Oil and gas storage; B. Petroleum products; C. Natural gas; D. Coal; E. Lumber; F. Building material; G. Construction equipment; H. Empty solid waste vehicles and containers; or I. Building materials.
sources.full_text: Industrial Storage: A fixed installation where any of the following are stored, either for subsequent transshipment to a smaller fixed installation or for pick-up by truck for transport to the site where the product will be used by the consumer: A. Oil and gas storage; B. Petroleum products; C. Natural gas; D. Coal; E. Lumber; F. Building material; G. Construction equipment; H. Empty solid waste vehicles and containers; or I. Building materials.

sources.title: Industrial/Production Use
sources.excerpt: Industrial/Production Use: A use classification that includes nonresidential and noncommercial employment uses such as, but not limited to, mining, milling and manufacturing.
sources.full_text: Industrial/Production Use: A use classification that includes nonresidential and noncommercial employment uses such as, but not limited to, mining, milling and manufacturing.

sources.title: Infrastructure
sources.excerpt: Infrastructure: A use classification that includes the basic installations and facilities on which new development depends. The public infrastructure includes public roads and public water and sewer lines.
sources.full_text: Infrastructure: A use classification that includes the basic installations and facilities on which new development depends. The public infrastructure includes public roads and public water and sewer lines.

sources.title: Inoperable Vehicle
sources.excerpt: Inoperable Vehicle: A motorcycle or motor vehicle, trailer, or attachment thereto that is required by the Commonwealth of Virginia to display current license plates and/or meet safety standards, as evidenced by display of an approved inspection sticker, which motorcycle, vehicle, trailer, or attachment therefore does not display said license plates and/or approved inspection sticker.
sources.full_text: Inoperable Vehicle: A motorcycle or motor vehicle, trailer, or attachment thereto that is required by the Commonwealth of Virginia to display current license plates and/or meet safety standards, as evidenced by display of an approved inspection sticker, which motorcycle, vehicle, trailer, or attachment therefore does not display said license plates and/or approved inspection sticker.

sources.title: Institutional Use
sources.excerpt: Institutional Use: A use category that includes public or private health, recreational, or educational uses such as parks, schools, libraries, hospitals, and camps.
sources.full_text: Institutional Use: A use category that includes public or private health, recreational, or educational uses such as parks, schools, libraries, hospitals, and camps.

sources.title: Intensity
sources.excerpt: Intensity: Physical measures of the scope and scale of land use, including building height, bulk, and coverage. The most common measurement of intensity is Floor Area Ratio (FAR).
sources.full_text: Intensity: Physical measures of the scope and scale of land use, including building height, bulk, and coverage. The most common measurement of intensity is Floor Area Ratio (FAR).

sources.title: Interactive Science & Technology Center
sources.excerpt: Interactive Science & Technology Center: See Cultural Facility.
sources.full_text: Interactive Science & Technology Center: See Cultural Facility.

sources.title: Invasive Plant
sources.excerpt: Invasive Plant: Plant species intentionally or accidentally introduced by human activity into a region in which they did not evolve and cause harm to natural resources, economic activity, or humans, plus any plant from any of the following categories. A. Plants shown on the Virginia Department of Conservation and Recreation's Virginia Invasive Plant Species List. B. Plants shown on the United States Department of Agriculture's official source for invasive or noxious plant species.
sources.full_text: Invasive Plant: Plant species intentionally or accidentally introduced by human activity into a region in which they did not evolve and cause harm to natural resources, economic activity, or humans, plus any plant from any of the following categories. A. Plants shown on the Virginia Department of Conservation and Recreation's Virginia Invasive Plant Species List. B. Plants shown on the United States Department of Agriculture's official source for invasive or noxious plant species.

sources.title: Junkyard
sources.excerpt: Junkyard: Any "automobile graveyard" or "junkyard" as defined by Code of Virginia § 33.2-804 excluding sanitary landfills or garbage dumps.
sources.full_text: Junkyard: Any "automobile graveyard" or "junkyard" as defined by Code of Virginia § 33.2-804 excluding sanitary landfills or garbage dumps.

sources.title: Karst/Sensitive Environmental Feature Setback
sources.excerpt: Karst/Sensitive Environmental Feature Setback: An area established from the edge of a karst/sensitive environmental feature, in which land disturbance is restricted.
sources.full_text: Karst/Sensitive Environmental Feature Setback: An area established from the edge of a karst/sensitive environmental feature, in which land disturbance is restricted.

sources.title: Karst Features or Karst/Sensitive Environmental Features
sources.excerpt: Karst Features or Karst/Sensitive Environmental Features: Karst landforms including but not limited to caves, sinkholes, rock outcrops, perennial sinking streams, significant fissures/cracks, vadose shafts, or other karst anomaly associated with calcareous geologic formations.
sources.full_text: Karst Features or Karst/Sensitive Environmental Features: Karst landforms including but not limited to caves, sinkholes, rock outcrops, perennial sinking streams, significant fissures/cracks, vadose shafts, or other karst anomaly associated with calcareous geologic formations.
sources.section_ref: Karst Features or Karst/Sensitive Environmental Features

sources.title: Karst Terrain
sources.excerpt: Karst Terrain: A type of terrain characterized by closed depressions and/or sinkholes, caves, rock pinnacles, and underground drainage, resulting from solution of limestone and dolomite bedrock.
sources.full_text: Karst Terrain: A type of terrain characterized by closed depressions and/or sinkholes, caves, rock pinnacles, and underground drainage, resulting from solution of limestone and dolomite bedrock.

sources.title: Kennel
sources.excerpt: Kennel: Any establishment or use in or at which 5 or more companion animals or pets over the age of 6 months are trained, fostered, boarded (including day care services), held for adoption, or handled. A kennel does not include any establishment whose principal use is grooming, any animal hospital, or pet shop.
sources.full_text: Kennel: Any establishment or use in or at which 5 or more companion animals or pets over the age of 6 months are trained, fostered, boarded (including day care services), held for adoption, or handled. A kennel does not include any establishment whose principal use is grooming, any animal hospital, or pet shop.

sources.title: Kennel, Indoor
sources.excerpt: Kennel, Indoor: Any kennel that is within a completely enclosed commercial facility with no outdoor activity in which companion animals are confined or penned proximate to each other, except for the primary purpose of grooming, or wherein any owner engages in boarding, breeding, letting for hire, training for a fee, or selling dogs, cats, or other companion animals.
sources.full_text: Kennel, Indoor: Any kennel that is within a completely enclosed commercial facility with no outdoor activity in which companion animals are confined or penned proximate to each other, except for the primary purpose of grooming, or wherein any owner engages in boarding, breeding, letting for hire, training for a fee, or selling dogs, cats, or other companion animals.

sources.title: Landfill Water Service District
sources.excerpt: Landfill Water Service District: A specific area designated for water service by central water supply system, the boundaries of which are determined by the County of Loudoun as shown on the Loudoun County Sanitation Authority Water and Sewer Lines map.
sources.full_text: Landfill Water Service District: A specific area designated for water service by central water supply system, the boundaries of which are determined by the County of Loudoun as shown on the Loudoun County Sanitation Authority Water and Sewer Lines map.

sources.title: Landfill, Sanitary
sources.excerpt: Landfill, Sanitary: A publicly owned and controlled, engineered land burial facility for the collection, source separation, storage, transportation, transfer, processing, treatment, or disposal of solid waste. Associated uses may include a debris landfill, a transfer station, Materials Recovery Facility (MRF), vegetative waste management facility, and/or an incinerator. It must mean a facility so located, designed, and operated that it does not impose a present or potential hazard to human healt
sources.full_text: Landfill, Sanitary: A publicly owned and controlled, engineered land burial facility for the collection, source separation, storage, transportation, transfer, processing, treatment, or disposal of solid waste. Associated uses may include a debris landfill, a transfer station, Materials Recovery Facility (MRF), vegetative waste management facility, and/or an incinerator. It must mean a facility so located, designed, and operated that it does not impose a present or potential hazard to human health or the environment, including pollution of air, land, surface water, or ground water.

sources.title: Landscape Architect
sources.excerpt: Landscape Architect: An individual who is recognized by the State and who is registered with the state department of professional and occupational registration as a "landscape architect".
sources.full_text: Landscape Architect: An individual who is recognized by the State and who is registered with the state department of professional and occupational registration as a "landscape architect".

sources.title: Landscaped Open Space
sources.excerpt: Landscaped Open Space: An area that must include appropriate grassing of the area, placement of shrubbery and trees, walkways, and appropriate grading so as to render the required open space area aesthetically pleasing.
sources.full_text: Landscaped Open Space: An area that must include appropriate grassing of the area, placement of shrubbery and trees, walkways, and appropriate grading so as to render the required open space area aesthetically pleasing.

sources.title: Land Subdivision and Development Ordinance (LSDO)
sources.excerpt: Land Subdivision and Development Ordinance: The Land Subdivision and Development Ordinance of Loudoun County, adopted by the Board of Supervisors to establish subdivision and site development plan standards and procedures for the unincorporated areas of Loudoun County.
sources.full_text: Land Subdivision and Development Ordinance: The Land Subdivision and Development Ordinance of Loudoun County, adopted by the Board of Supervisors to establish subdivision and site development plan standards and procedures for the unincorporated areas of Loudoun County.

sources.title: Ldn
sources.excerpt: Ldn: The term for "yearly day-night average sound level", which means the 365-day average, in decibels, for the period from midnight to midnight, obtained after the addition of 10 decibels to sound levels for the periods between 10 p.m. and 7 a.m.
sources.full_text: Ldn: The term for "yearly day-night average sound level", which means the 365-day average, in decibels, for the period from midnight to midnight, obtained after the addition of 10 decibels to sound levels for the periods between 10 p.m. and 7 a.m.

sources.title: Ldn 45 db(A)
sources.excerpt: Ldn 45 db(A): The term for the required level of noise attenuation in residential structures, expressing a required yearly interior day-night average sound level of 45 decibels or less.
sources.full_text: Ldn 45 db(A): The term for the required level of noise attenuation in residential structures, expressing a required yearly interior day-night average sound level of 45 decibels or less.

sources.title: Legacy Zoning District
sources.excerpt: Legacy Zoning District: An existing zoning district that does not align with Place Types of the General Plan that is retained to continue implementing the zoning district existing prior to adoption of this Zoning Ordinance (December 13, 2023) and avoid using more than 1 Zoning Ordinance.
sources.full_text: Legacy Zoning District: An existing zoning district that does not align with Place Types of the General Plan that is retained to continue implementing the zoning district existing prior to adoption of this Zoning Ordinance (December 13, 2023) and avoid using more than 1 Zoning Ordinance.

sources.title: Library
sources.excerpt: Library: Establishments that maintain collections of documents (e.g., books, journals, newspapers, music, and recorded information regardless of its physical form and characteristics) and facilitate their use to meet the informational, research, educational, or recreational needs of the general public or their user. These establishments may also acquire, research, store, preserve, and generally make accessible to the public historical documents, photographs, maps, audio material, audiovisual mat
sources.full_text: Library: Establishments that maintain collections of documents (e.g., books, journals, newspapers, music, and recorded information regardless of its physical form and characteristics) and facilitate their use to meet the informational, research, educational, or recreational needs of the general public or their user. These establishments may also acquire, research, store, preserve, and generally make accessible to the public historical documents, photographs, maps, audio material, audiovisual material, and other archival material of historical interest. All or portions of these collections may be accessed electronically.

sources.title: Light Post Banner
sources.excerpt: Light Post Banner: See Sign, Light Post Banner.
sources.full_text: Light Post Banner: See Sign, Light Post Banner.

sources.title: Limestone
sources.excerpt: Limestone: Geologic formation that is highly water-soluble and is characterized by numerous underground caves and surface sinkholes; it is a natural groundwater aquifer and good water supply source. Limestone consists of a conglomerate of various small rocks cemented together with a carbonate matrix and containing calcareous geologic formations. In appearance, it is very similar to concrete.
sources.full_text: Limestone: Geologic formation that is highly water-soluble and is characterized by numerous underground caves and surface sinkholes; it is a natural groundwater aquifer and good water supply source. Limestone consists of a conglomerate of various small rocks cemented together with a carbonate matrix and containing calcareous geologic formations. In appearance, it is very similar to concrete.

sources.title: Livestock
sources.excerpt: Livestock: Animals, especially farm animals, raised for use, profit, or enjoyment including horses and very small equine, bison, cattle, pigs, mules, sheep, goats, alpacas, llamas, emus, poultry (including egg-producing poultry), and other similar domesticated animals. Livestock does not include animals meeting the definition of Companion Animal.
sources.full_text: Livestock: Animals, especially farm animals, raised for use, profit, or enjoyment including horses and very small equine, bison, cattle, pigs, mules, sheep, goats, alpacas, llamas, emus, poultry (including egg-producing poultry), and other similar domesticated animals. Livestock does not include animals meeting the definition of Companion Animal.

sources.title: Live/Work Dwelling
sources.excerpt: Live/Work Dwelling: See "Dwelling, Live/Work."
sources.full_text: Live/Work Dwelling: See "Dwelling, Live/Work."

sources.title: Limestone Overlay District
sources.excerpt: Limestone Overlay District: Limestone Overlay District, as defined in Chapter 5.05 of the Zoning Ordinance.
sources.full_text: Limestone Overlay District: Limestone Overlay District, as defined in Chapter 5.05 of the Zoning Ordinance.

sources.title: Lodging
sources.excerpt: Lodging: A use classification composed of uses intended for living and sleeping only on a transient basis.
sources.full_text: Lodging: A use classification composed of uses intended for living and sleeping only on a transient basis.

sources.title: Lodging Unit
sources.excerpt: Lodging Unit: Rooms or connected group of rooms forming a single habitable unit used or intended to be used for living and sleeping only on a transient basis. Each lodging unit must have its own bathroom and may contain independent kitchen facilities.
sources.full_text: Lodging Unit: Rooms or connected group of rooms forming a single habitable unit used or intended to be used for living and sleeping only on a transient basis. Each lodging unit must have its own bathroom and may contain independent kitchen facilities.

sources.title: Lot
sources.excerpt: Lot: A parcel of subdivided land occupied, or to be occupied, by 1 or more buildings and accessory buildings, and open space, and other requirements of the provisions of the Zoning Ordinance. "Lot" includes the words "plot" and "parcel". The term "lot" does not include land identified on a legally recorded subdivision plat filed among the land records of the circuit court, as an "outlot" as referenced in Section 1.03.04.A.4. Figure 12.L-1. Lot Types
sources.full_text: Lot: A parcel of subdivided land occupied, or to be occupied, by 1 or more buildings and accessory buildings, and open space, and other requirements of the provisions of the Zoning Ordinance. "Lot" includes the words "plot" and "parcel". The term "lot" does not include land identified on a legally recorded subdivision plat filed among the land records of the circuit court, as an "outlot" as referenced in Section 1.03.04.A.4. Figure 12.L-1. Lot Types

sources.title: Lot Area
