sources.full_text: Sinkhole: A vertical opening or closed depression in the land surface and formed by solution of carbonate bedrock and downward movement of soil into bedrock voids or by collapse of underlying caves.

sources.title: Sinking Stream
sources.excerpt: Sinking Stream: Any stream draining 640 acres or less that disappears underground into a Swallet, Sinkhole, Closed Depression or Cave Opening.
sources.full_text: Sinking Stream: Any stream draining 640 acres or less that disappears underground into a Swallet, Sinkhole, Closed Depression or Cave Opening.

sources.title: Site Plan
sources.excerpt: Site Plan: A required submission, prepared and approved in accordance with the provisions of Chapter 10.06, that is a detailed engineering drawing of the proposed improvements included and required in the development of a given lot. A Site Plan is not to be construed as a Concept Development Plan, as required by other provisions of the Zoning Ordinance. Reference the Site Plan Procedures of the Land Subdivision and Development Ordinance (LSDO).
sources.full_text: Site Plan: A required submission, prepared and approved in accordance with the provisions of Chapter 10.06, that is a detailed engineering drawing of the proposed improvements included and required in the development of a given lot. A Site Plan is not to be construed as a Concept Development Plan, as required by other provisions of the Zoning Ordinance. Reference the Site Plan Procedures of the Land Subdivision and Development Ordinance (LSDO).

sources.title: Slaughterhouse
sources.excerpt: Slaughterhouse: Any structure or land where livestock raised off-site are: (i) slaughtered; (ii) preparing processed meats and meat byproducts; or (iii) rendered and/or refined animal fat, bones, and meat scraps. This includes establishments primarily engaged in assembly cutting and packing of meats (i.e., boxed meats) from purchased carcasses.
sources.full_text: Slaughterhouse: Any structure or land where livestock raised off-site are: (i) slaughtered; (ii) preparing processed meats and meat byproducts; or (iii) rendered and/or refined animal fat, bones, and meat scraps. This includes establishments primarily engaged in assembly cutting and packing of meats (i.e., boxed meats) from purchased carcasses.

sources.title: Slope
sources.excerpt: Slope: The vertical elevation of land area divided by the horizontal distance, expressed as a percentage. Slope percentage must be determined using the County base planimetric and topographic maps or if required, then other topographic maps, elevations, etc., prepared by such persons licensed to perform surveys to determine such information. See Very Steep Slope Area and Moderately Steep Slope Area.
sources.full_text: Slope: The vertical elevation of land area divided by the horizontal distance, expressed as a percentage. Slope percentage must be determined using the County base planimetric and topographic maps or if required, then other topographic maps, elevations, etc., prepared by such persons licensed to perform surveys to determine such information. See Very Steep Slope Area and Moderately Steep Slope Area.

sources.title: Small Business, Agricultural and Rural
sources.excerpt: Small Business, Agricultural and Rural: A business, profession, occupation, or trade established as a principal use, subject to Section 4.04.19. Small Business uses must not include the following elements: A. Franchises. B. Branch Facilities. C. Partial elements of larger enterprises that have other business facilities in other locations.
sources.full_text: Small Business, Agricultural and Rural: A business, profession, occupation, or trade established as a principal use, subject to Section 4.04.19. Small Business uses must not include the following elements: A. Franchises. B. Branch Facilities. C. Partial elements of larger enterprises that have other business facilities in other locations.

sources.title: Snack Bar or Refreshment Stand, Accessory
sources.excerpt: Snack Bar or Refreshment Stand, Accessory: An accessory use that prepares and/or serves snacks and non-alcoholic beverages for consumption on the premises and is operated solely by and for the agency, group, or owner operating the principal use and for the convenience of patrons of the facility.
sources.full_text: Snack Bar or Refreshment Stand, Accessory: An accessory use that prepares and/or serves snacks and non-alcoholic beverages for consumption on the premises and is operated solely by and for the agency, group, or owner operating the principal use and for the convenience of patrons of the facility.
sources.section_ref: Snack Bar or Refreshment Stand, Accessory

sources.title: Soil Mapping Unit
sources.excerpt: Soil Mapping Unit: An area of similar kinds of soil materials on the same landform, as defined in the Interpretive Guide to Soils Maps of Loudoun County, as amended.
sources.full_text: Soil Mapping Unit: An area of similar kinds of soil materials on the same landform, as defined in the Interpretive Guide to Soils Maps of Loudoun County, as amended.

sources.title: Solar Facility
sources.excerpt: Solar Facility: A facility for the primary purpose of collecting, generating, and/or transferring electric energy from solar radiation (i.e., sunlight). Includes without limitation any ground-mounted solar energy generation facility.
sources.full_text: Solar Facility: A facility for the primary purpose of collecting, generating, and/or transferring electric energy from solar radiation (i.e., sunlight). Includes without limitation any ground-mounted solar energy generation facility.

sources.title: Solar Facility, Utility Scale
sources.excerpt: Solar Facility, Utility Scale: A solar facility for the primary purpose of supplying energy to the power grid. Such facility may include energy storage, utility lines, and/or a utility substation.
sources.full_text: Solar Facility, Utility Scale: A solar facility for the primary purpose of supplying energy to the power grid. Such facility may include energy storage, utility lines, and/or a utility substation.

sources.title: Solar Facility, Site-Specific
sources.excerpt: Solar Facility, Site-Specific: A solar facility for the primary purpose of supplying energy to a principal use on the same site and that such solar facility is accessory to.
sources.full_text: Solar Facility, Site-Specific: A solar facility for the primary purpose of supplying energy to a principal use on the same site and that such solar facility is accessory to.

sources.title: Solid Waste
sources.excerpt: Solid Waste: Any garbage, refuse, sludge, and other discarded materials, resulting from household, industrial, commercial, mining, and agricultural operations, or community activities, but not including (i) solid or dissolved material in domestic sewage, (ii) solid or dissolved material in irrigation return flows or in industrial discharges that are sources subject to a permit from the State Water Control Board, or (iii) source, special nuclear, or by-product material as defined by the Federal A
sources.full_text: Solid Waste: Any garbage, refuse, sludge, and other discarded materials, resulting from household, industrial, commercial, mining, and agricultural operations, or community activities, but not including (i) solid or dissolved material in domestic sewage, (ii) solid or dissolved material in irrigation return flows or in industrial discharges that are sources subject to a permit from the State Water Control Board, or (iii) source, special nuclear, or by-product material as defined by the Federal Atomic Energy Act of 1954, as amended.

sources.title: Solid Waste Container
sources.excerpt: Solid Waste Container: A container that is specifically designed, constructed, and placed for use as a depository for the storage of solid waste, which includes dumpsters, bulk containers, refuse containers, or other such containers specifically designated as waste receptacles.
sources.full_text: Solid Waste Container: A container that is specifically designed, constructed, and placed for use as a depository for the storage of solid waste, which includes dumpsters, bulk containers, refuse containers, or other such containers specifically designated as waste receptacles.

sources.title: Solid Waste Facility
sources.excerpt: Solid Waste Facility: Any "solid waste management facility" or "sanitary landfill" as defined by the Virginia Waste Management Act (Code of Virginia § 10.1-1400). This use includes Landfill, sanitary; and Garbage incinerating, reducing, or dumping for compensation, including loading or transfer.
sources.full_text: Solid Waste Facility: Any "solid waste management facility" or "sanitary landfill" as defined by the Virginia Waste Management Act (Code of Virginia § 10.1-1400). This use includes Landfill, sanitary; and Garbage incinerating, reducing, or dumping for compensation, including loading or transfer.

sources.title: Solid Waste Incinerator
sources.excerpt: Solid Waste Incinerator: A facility or device designed for the treatment of solid waste by combustion.
sources.full_text: Solid Waste Incinerator: A facility or device designed for the treatment of solid waste by combustion.

sources.title: Solid Waste Transfer Station
sources.excerpt: Solid Waste Transfer Station: A solid waste collection or storage facility at which solid waste is transferred from collection vehicles to hauling vehicles for transportation to a central solid waste management facility for disposal, incineration, or resource recovery.
sources.full_text: Solid Waste Transfer Station: A solid waste collection or storage facility at which solid waste is transferred from collection vehicles to hauling vehicles for transportation to a central solid waste management facility for disposal, incineration, or resource recovery.

sources.title: Sound
sources.excerpt: Sound: An oscillation in pressure, particle displacement, particle velocity, or other physical parameter, in a medium with internal forces that causes compression and rarefaction of that medium.
sources.full_text: Sound: An oscillation in pressure, particle displacement, particle velocity, or other physical parameter, in a medium with internal forces that causes compression and rarefaction of that medium.

sources.title: Sound Level Meter
sources.excerpt: Sound Level Meter: Means an electronic instrument, which includes a microphone, an amplifier and an output meter, that measures noises and sound pressure levels in a specified manner. It may be used with the octave band analyzer that permits measuring the sound pressure level in discrete octave bands.
sources.full_text: Sound Level Meter: Means an electronic instrument, which includes a microphone, an amplifier and an output meter, that measures noises and sound pressure levels in a specified manner. It may be used with the octave band analyzer that permits measuring the sound pressure level in discrete octave bands.

sources.title: Special Event
sources.excerpt: Special Event: A temporary commercial or festive activity or promotion at a specific location that is open to the public and is planned or reasonably expected to attract large assemblies of persons. Special events include, but are not limited to, carnivals, festivals, circuses, music fairs or concerts, tent revivals, art shows, crafts shows, rodeos, corn mazes, civil war reenactments, equestrian shows, firework displays and events, or similar events open to the public. A private party held at a
sources.full_text: Special Event: A temporary commercial or festive activity or promotion at a specific location that is open to the public and is planned or reasonably expected to attract large assemblies of persons. Special events include, but are not limited to, carnivals, festivals, circuses, music fairs or concerts, tent revivals, art shows, crafts shows, rodeos, corn mazes, civil war reenactments, equestrian shows, firework displays and events, or similar events open to the public. A private party held at a Banquet/Event Facility (including Hotel, Conference Center, Restaurant, Rural Resort, or similar facility), Bed and Breakfast Homestay, Bed and Breakfast Inn, or Country Inn is not a special event. In addition to events open to the public, a private party held at a location other than the foregoing or held on property not occupied by the host, is a special event. Special Event does not include temporary or seasonal retail sales of goods, products, or services, such as temporary sales of Christmas trees, farm produce, fireworks, and other similar seasonal goods.

sources.title: Special Exception
sources.excerpt: Special Exception: Special Exception applies to the following: A. Special Exception (Use): A use that may be allowed in a zoning district subject to conditions imposed by the Board of Supervisors, in accordance with the procedures and standards for Special Exceptions in Section 10.11; and B. Special Exception (Process): The process that provides the Board of Supervisors with an opportunity for discretionary review of requests to establish, construct, or modify uses or structures as described in
sources.full_text: Special Exception: Special Exception applies to the following: A. Special Exception (Use): A use that may be allowed in a zoning district subject to conditions imposed by the Board of Supervisors, in accordance with the procedures and standards for Special Exceptions in Section 10.11; and B. Special Exception (Process): The process that provides the Board of Supervisors with an opportunity for discretionary review of requests to establish, construct, or modify uses or structures as described in Section 10.11.

sources.title: Specimen Tree
sources.excerpt: Specimen Tree: Any tree that has been individually designated by the local governing body to be notable by virtue of its outstanding size and quality for its particular species.
sources.full_text: Specimen Tree: Any tree that has been individually designated by the local governing body to be notable by virtue of its outstanding size and quality for its particular species.

sources.title: Sports Stadium, Complex, Arena or Sports Field
sources.excerpt: Sports Stadium, Complex, Arena or Sports Field: See Recreation, Outdoor or Major.
sources.full_text: Sports Stadium, Complex, Arena or Sports Field: See Recreation, Outdoor or Major.
sources.section_ref: Sports Stadium, Complex, Arena or Sports Field

sources.title: Spring
sources.excerpt: Spring: A feature where the groundwater flows naturally from a rock or the soil onto the land surface or into a body of surface water.
sources.full_text: Spring: A feature where the groundwater flows naturally from a rock or the soil onto the land surface or into a body of surface water.

sources.title: Stable
sources.excerpt: Stable: An establishment that keeps or boards horses. A. Stable, Private: A facility that boards: 1. Horses for the private use of the owners and/or residents of the lot; and/or 2. No more than 10 horses not owned by the property owner or resident. 3. Private Stable includes the ability to teach students equitation. B. Stable, Livery: An establishment that boards: 1. More than 10 horses not directly owned by the property owner; 2. More than 10 horses used for hire; or 3. A combination thereof.
sources.full_text: Stable: An establishment that keeps or boards horses. A. Stable, Private: A facility that boards: 1. Horses for the private use of the owners and/or residents of the lot; and/or 2. No more than 10 horses not owned by the property owner or resident. 3. Private Stable includes the ability to teach students equitation. B. Stable, Livery: An establishment that boards: 1. More than 10 horses not directly owned by the property owner; 2. More than 10 horses used for hire; or 3. A combination thereof.

sources.title: State
sources.excerpt: State: The Commonwealth of Virginia.
sources.full_text: State: The Commonwealth of Virginia.

sources.title: Steep Slopes
sources.excerpt: Steep Slopes: Surface formation with a vertical incline greater than 15%, which is sufficient steepness to cause problems such as erosion or increased flooding when land is disturbed. See Moderately Steep Slopes and Very Steep Slopes.
sources.full_text: Steep Slopes: Surface formation with a vertical incline greater than 15%, which is sufficient steepness to cause problems such as erosion or increased flooding when land is disturbed. See Moderately Steep Slopes and Very Steep Slopes.

sources.title: Step-Back
sources.excerpt: Step-Back: An area of a façade above the first floor of a building or structure that is located a set distance recessed from the façade, story, or portion of the building or structure below it.
sources.full_text: Step-Back: An area of a façade above the first floor of a building or structure that is located a set distance recessed from the façade, story, or portion of the building or structure below it.

sources.title: Stockpiling
sources.excerpt: Stockpiling: The acceptance, collection, accumulation, or aggregation of dirt, fill, or gravel from off-site sources, for a period of more than 12 months.
sources.full_text: Stockpiling: The acceptance, collection, accumulation, or aggregation of dirt, fill, or gravel from off-site sources, for a period of more than 12 months.

sources.title: Stockpiling, Temporary
sources.excerpt: Stockpiling, Temporary: The acceptance, collection, accumulation or aggregation of dirt, fill, or gravel from off-site sources, for a period of fewer than 12 months on a parcel. Such stockpiles are removed or immediately graded out in accordance with an approved grading plan.
sources.full_text: Stockpiling, Temporary: The acceptance, collection, accumulation or aggregation of dirt, fill, or gravel from off-site sources, for a period of fewer than 12 months on a parcel. Such stockpiles are removed or immediately graded out in accordance with an approved grading plan.

sources.title: Stone Quarrying Industries
sources.excerpt: Stone Quarrying Industries: Commercial or industrial stone quarries or operations involving removal from a site of natural accumulations of sand, rock, soil, or gravel. The meaning of "stone quarrying", includes appurtenant structures such as crushers, screeners, and washers and also includes, as an accessory use, retail sales of stone products, but does not include any other industrial use, such as concrete batching plants or asphalt mixing plants.
sources.full_text: Stone Quarrying Industries: Commercial or industrial stone quarries or operations involving removal from a site of natural accumulations of sand, rock, soil, or gravel. The meaning of "stone quarrying", includes appurtenant structures such as crushers, screeners, and washers and also includes, as an accessory use, retail sales of stone products, but does not include any other industrial use, such as concrete batching plants or asphalt mixing plants.

sources.title: Storage Area
sources.excerpt: Storage Area: An outdoor space accessory to a permitted use, used for the purpose of storing equipment, vehicles, construction materials, and similar items necessary to the permitted use.
sources.full_text: Storage Area: An outdoor space accessory to a permitted use, used for the purpose of storing equipment, vehicles, construction materials, and similar items necessary to the permitted use.

sources.title: Storage, Bulk Gasoline
sources.excerpt: Storage, Bulk Gasoline: A fixed installation where these products are stored, either for subsequent transshipment to a smaller fixed installation or for pick-up by truck for transport to the site where the product will be used by the consumer. Materials may be stored in 1 or more tanks on the site, the size of a tank being constrained by Federal and State regulations pertaining to construction and safety standards. Such facilities do not include retail sales. Accessory tanks for agriculture, tem
sources.full_text: Storage, Bulk Gasoline: A fixed installation where these products are stored, either for subsequent transshipment to a smaller fixed installation or for pick-up by truck for transport to the site where the product will be used by the consumer. Materials may be stored in 1 or more tanks on the site, the size of a tank being constrained by Federal and State regulations pertaining to construction and safety standards. Such facilities do not include retail sales. Accessory tanks for agriculture, temporary construction, or personal use are excluded from this definition.

sources.title: Stormwater Management Improvements
sources.excerpt: Stormwater Management Improvements: Surface or subsurface drainage improvements, storm sewers, detention and retention ponds, and other such improvements as required by the Facilities Standards Manual (FSM) or the Loudoun County Stormwater Management Ordinance.
sources.full_text: Stormwater Management Improvements: Surface or subsurface drainage improvements, storm sewers, detention and retention ponds, and other such improvements as required by the Facilities Standards Manual (FSM) or the Loudoun County Stormwater Management Ordinance.

sources.title: Story
sources.excerpt: Story: That portion of a building included between the surface of any floor and the surface of the floor next above it or, if there be no floor above it, the space between such floor and the ceiling next above. A basement is counted as a story if it is used for business or dwelling purposes. A mezzanine floor is counted as a story if it covers more than one-third of the area of the floor next below it or if the vertical distance between the floor next below it and the floor next above it is 20 f
sources.full_text: Story: That portion of a building included between the surface of any floor and the surface of the floor next above it or, if there be no floor above it, the space between such floor and the ceiling next above. A basement is counted as a story if it is used for business or dwelling purposes. A mezzanine floor is counted as a story if it covers more than one-third of the area of the floor next below it or if the vertical distance between the floor next below it and the floor next above it is 20 feet or more.

sources.title: Stream Bank
sources.excerpt: Stream Bank: The confining cut of land forming a natural channel for the nontidal water movement of a stream.
sources.full_text: Stream Bank: The confining cut of land forming a natural channel for the nontidal water movement of a stream.

sources.title: Stream Restoration
sources.excerpt: Stream Restoration: A process designed in accordance with the Facilities Standards Manual (FSM) and approved by the County. An increase in base flood elevation may be permitted provided a Conditional Letter of Map Revision (CLOMR) is obtained from the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) prior to approval of the requisite Floodplain Alteration application for such use.
sources.full_text: Stream Restoration: A process designed in accordance with the Facilities Standards Manual (FSM) and approved by the County. An increase in base flood elevation may be permitted provided a Conditional Letter of Map Revision (CLOMR) is obtained from the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) prior to approval of the requisite Floodplain Alteration application for such use.

sources.title: Street, Centerline of
sources.excerpt: Street, Centerline of: A line established as a centerline of a street by any State, County, or other official agency or governing body having jurisdiction thereof and shown as such on an officially adopted or legally recorded map or, if there be no official centerline, a line running and lying midway between the street right-of-way lines. Where street lines are indeterminate and a pavement or a traveled way exists, the centerline is assumed to be a line midway between the edges of such pavement
sources.full_text: Street, Centerline of: A line established as a centerline of a street by any State, County, or other official agency or governing body having jurisdiction thereof and shown as such on an officially adopted or legally recorded map or, if there be no official centerline, a line running and lying midway between the street right-of-way lines. Where street lines are indeterminate and a pavement or a traveled way exists, the centerline is assumed to be a line midway between the edges of such pavement or traveled way.

sources.title: Streetlight
sources.excerpt: Streetlight: A pole or pedestal mounted luminaire with a metal halide or other full spectrum bulb.
sources.full_text: Streetlight: A pole or pedestal mounted luminaire with a metal halide or other full spectrum bulb.

sources.title: Street Tree
sources.excerpt: Street Tree: Any tree that has been individually designated by the local governing body and grows in the street right-of-way or on private property as authorized by the owner and placed or planted there by the local government.
sources.full_text: Street Tree: Any tree that has been individually designated by the local governing body and grows in the street right-of-way or on private property as authorized by the owner and placed or planted there by the local government.

sources.title: Structural Alteration
sources.excerpt: Structural Alteration: Any change, modification, addition, or deletion to the bearing walls of an existing structure.
sources.full_text: Structural Alteration: Any change, modification, addition, or deletion to the bearing walls of an existing structure.

sources.title: Structure
sources.excerpt: Structure: An assembly of materials forming a construction for occupancy or use including, among others, buildings, stadiums, tents, parking lots, platforms, stagings, observation towers, telecommunications towers, radio and television broadcasting towers, water tanks, trestles, piers, swimming pools, open sheds, shelters, walls, power line towers, and pipelines. For the purposes of Sections 5.04 and 5.05, the term “structure” includes, but is not limited to, all buildings, water/dam embankments
sources.full_text: Structure: An assembly of materials forming a construction for occupancy or use including, among others, buildings, stadiums, tents, parking lots, platforms, stagings, observation towers, telecommunications towers, radio and television broadcasting towers, water tanks, trestles, piers, swimming pools, open sheds, shelters, walls, power line towers, and pipelines. For the purposes of Sections 5.04 and 5.05, the term “structure” includes, but is not limited to, all buildings, water/dam embankments, retaining walls, field/tile drainage, road construction, and cut or fill operations.

sources.title: Structure, Accessory
sources.excerpt: Structure, Accessory: A building or structure subordinate and incidental to, and located on the same lot with, a principal building, the use of which is customarily found in association with and is clearly incidental to that of the main building or to the use of the land, and is not attached by any part of a common wall or roof to the principal building.
sources.full_text: Structure, Accessory: A building or structure subordinate and incidental to, and located on the same lot with, a principal building, the use of which is customarily found in association with and is clearly incidental to that of the main building or to the use of the land, and is not attached by any part of a common wall or roof to the principal building.

sources.title: Subdivision
sources.excerpt: Subdivision: The division of a parcel of land into 2 or more new parcels. The process of subdividing is regulated by the Land Subdivision and Development Ordinance.
sources.full_text: Subdivision: The division of a parcel of land into 2 or more new parcels. The process of subdividing is regulated by the Land Subdivision and Development Ordinance.

sources.title: Subdivision Plat
sources.excerpt: Subdivision Plat: A record plat for a subdivision. See Record Plat.
sources.full_text: Subdivision Plat: A record plat for a subdivision. See Record Plat.

sources.title: Subordinate Lot
sources.excerpt: Subordinate Lot: One of the types of lots permitted within the Principal/Subordinate Subdivision Development Option. Subordinate Lots may not be further subdivided.
sources.full_text: Subordinate Lot: One of the types of lots permitted within the Principal/Subordinate Subdivision Development Option. Subordinate Lots may not be further subdivided.

sources.title: Substantial Conformance
sources.excerpt: Substantial Conformance: Substantial conformance is determined by the Zoning Administrator and means conformance that leaves a reasonable margin for adjustment due to final design or engineering data but conforms with the general nature of the development, the specific uses, and the general layout depicted by the plans, profiles, elevations, and other demonstrative materials proffered by the applicant.
sources.full_text: Substantial Conformance: Substantial conformance is determined by the Zoning Administrator and means conformance that leaves a reasonable margin for adjustment due to final design or engineering data but conforms with the general nature of the development, the specific uses, and the general layout depicted by the plans, profiles, elevations, and other demonstrative materials proffered by the applicant.

sources.title: Swallet
sources.excerpt: Swallet: A hole in the land through which surface water is delivered to the aquifer (may be considered the opposite of a spring).
sources.full_text: Swallet: A hole in the land through which surface water is delivered to the aquifer (may be considered the opposite of a spring).

sources.title: Telecommunications Facility
sources.excerpt: Telecommunications Facility: A facility utilized by a public service utility or commercial public telecommunications service under the jurisdiction of the Virginia State Corporation Commission and/or licensed by the Federal Communications Commission to provide commercial public telecommunications services. A telecommunications facility may include a tower, monopole, antenna hub site, and other antenna support structures or equipment buildings. A telecommunications facility does not include non-c
sources.full_text: Telecommunications Facility: A facility utilized by a public service utility or commercial public telecommunications service under the jurisdiction of the Virginia State Corporation Commission and/or licensed by the Federal Communications Commission to provide commercial public telecommunications services. A telecommunications facility may include a tower, monopole, antenna hub site, and other antenna support structures or equipment buildings. A telecommunications facility does not include non-commercial applications, such as amateur radio operations, or uses or structures that are accessory to and solely used by an individual business. A. Antenna, Telecommunications: Any structure or device used to collect or radiate electromagnetic waves, including both directional antennas, such as panels and microwave dishes, and omnidirectional antennas, such as whips and satellite dishes, but not including satellite earth stations. A telecommunications antenna includes an antenna hub site. B. Antenna Hub Site: An area containing 1 or more unmanned equipment structures sited to serve telecommunications antennas. An antenna hub site may be located on a different lot than the telecommunications antennas served. C. Antenna Support Structure: A self-supporting or bracketed ground-mounted pole 60 feet or less in height that is accessory to and used to support 1 or more telecommunications antennas and no more than 1 related unmanned equipment structure. D. Monopole, Telecommunications: A single, self-supporting pole-type structure, tapering from base to top and supporting a fixture designed to hold 1 or more telecommunications antennas. A monopole does not include a telecommunications tower. E. Tower, Telecommunications: A lattice-type structure, guyed or self-supporting, used to support telecommunications antennas. Does not include a radio, radar and/or television tower. F. Temporary Mobile and Land-Based Telecommunication Testing Facility: Whip antennas, panels antennas, microwave dishes, and receive-only satellite dishes and related equipment for wireless audio-transmission with low wattage not to exceed 500 watts, from a sender to 1 or more receivers, such as for mobile cellular telephones and mobile radio system facilities.

sources.title: Temporary Retail
sources.excerpt: Temporary Retail: A temporary use in which stands or sales areas are set aside and rented or otherwise provided, and that are intended for use by various unrelated individuals or entities to sell articles that are primarily homemade, homegrown, handcrafted, old, obsolete, or antique and may include the selling of goods at retail by businesses or individuals who are generally engaged in retail trade.
sources.full_text: Temporary Retail: A temporary use in which stands or sales areas are set aside and rented or otherwise provided, and that are intended for use by various unrelated individuals or entities to sell articles that are primarily homemade, homegrown, handcrafted, old, obsolete, or antique and may include the selling of goods at retail by businesses or individuals who are generally engaged in retail trade.

sources.title: Theater
sources.excerpt: Theater: An establishment engaged in the commercial exhibition of motion pictures to the general public or used for the performance of theatrical productions, plays, acts, dramas by actors and/or actresses. This use includes a performing arts center.
sources.full_text: Theater: An establishment engaged in the commercial exhibition of motion pictures to the general public or used for the performance of theatrical productions, plays, acts, dramas by actors and/or actresses. This use includes a performing arts center.

sources.title: Toll
sources.excerpt: Toll: To pause or delay the running of a period of time set forth by statute or the Zoning Ordinance.
sources.full_text: Toll: To pause or delay the running of a period of time set forth by statute or the Zoning Ordinance.

sources.title: Town Center Core
sources.excerpt: Town Center Core: Within the Town Center (TC) Zoning District, a zone that provides a concentration of vertically and horizontally integrated mixed-use buildings, multistory single-use buildings, and pedestrian-oriented design located proximate to each other to generate and support pedestrian activity.
sources.full_text: Town Center Core: Within the Town Center (TC) Zoning District, a zone that provides a concentration of vertically and horizontally integrated mixed-use buildings, multistory single-use buildings, and pedestrian-oriented design located proximate to each other to generate and support pedestrian activity.

sources.title: Town Center Fringe
sources.excerpt: Town Center Fringe: Within the Town Center (TC) Zoning District, the transition area located between the Town Center Core and surrounding development and includes pedestrian-oriented businesses and activity balance with Residential and other uses more dependent on vehicular access.
sources.full_text: Town Center Fringe: Within the Town Center (TC) Zoning District, the transition area located between the Town Center Core and surrounding development and includes pedestrian-oriented businesses and activity balance with Residential and other uses more dependent on vehicular access.

sources.title: Town Green
sources.excerpt: Town Green: See Green.
sources.full_text: Town Green: See Green.

sources.title: Trails
sources.excerpt: Trails: A marked or established path or route for non-motorized recreational use. Examples include, but are not limited to footpaths, raised boardwalks, biking or hiking paths, and horse trails.
sources.full_text: Trails: A marked or established path or route for non-motorized recreational use. Examples include, but are not limited to footpaths, raised boardwalks, biking or hiking paths, and horse trails.

sources.title: Transit Facilities
sources.excerpt: Transit Facilities: Includes transit stations, bus terminals, and associated right-of-way. Accessory uses include vehicle and bicycle parking.
sources.full_text: Transit Facilities: Includes transit stations, bus terminals, and associated right-of-way. Accessory uses include vehicle and bicycle parking.

sources.title: Travelway
sources.excerpt: Travelway: Part of a road for vehicle use.
sources.full_text: Travelway: Part of a road for vehicle use.

sources.title: Tree
sources.excerpt: Tree: A single perennial woody stem attaining a height of 15 feet or more at maturity.
sources.full_text: Tree: A single perennial woody stem attaining a height of 15 feet or more at maturity.

sources.title: Tree Canopy
sources.excerpt: Tree Canopy: The area projected to be directly beneath the crown and within the dripline of a given tree species after a 10-year or 20-year growing period that is counted to fulfill canopy requirements.
sources.full_text: Tree Canopy: The area projected to be directly beneath the crown and within the dripline of a given tree species after a 10-year or 20-year growing period that is counted to fulfill canopy requirements.

sources.title: Tree Cover
sources.excerpt: Tree Cover: The area directly beneath the crown and within the dripline of a given tree species or forested areas.
sources.full_text: Tree Cover: The area directly beneath the crown and within the dripline of a given tree species or forested areas.

sources.title: Tree, Deciduous
sources.excerpt: Tree, Deciduous: Trees and shrubs that shed their leaves annually, usually in autumn.
sources.full_text: Tree, Deciduous: Trees and shrubs that shed their leaves annually, usually in autumn.

sources.title: Tree, Evergreen
sources.excerpt: Tree, Evergreen: A non-deciduous tree, often used for the purposes of screening, weather barrier, or accent planting. Acceptable species are provided in the Facilities Standards Manual.
sources.full_text: Tree, Evergreen: A non-deciduous tree, often used for the purposes of screening, weather barrier, or accent planting. Acceptable species are provided in the Facilities Standards Manual.

sources.title: Tree, Large Deciduous
sources.excerpt: Tree, Large Deciduous: A deciduous tree, usually single trunked, which typically attains a mature height of at least 30 feet. Acceptable species are provided in the Facilities Standards Manual.
sources.full_text: Tree, Large Deciduous: A deciduous tree, usually single trunked, which typically attains a mature height of at least 30 feet. Acceptable species are provided in the Facilities Standards Manual.

sources.title: Tree, Small Deciduous
sources.excerpt: Tree, Small Deciduous: A deciduous tree that does not typically attain a mature height of greater than 30 feet. Acceptable species are provided in the Facilities Standards Manual.
sources.full_text: Tree, Small Deciduous: A deciduous tree that does not typically attain a mature height of greater than 30 feet. Acceptable species are provided in the Facilities Standards Manual.

sources.title: Undisturbed Grade
sources.excerpt: Undisturbed Grade: The grade and elevation of land prior to excavation, filling, or grading.
sources.full_text: Undisturbed Grade: The grade and elevation of land prior to excavation, filling, or grading.

sources.title: University
sources.excerpt: University: See College or University.
sources.full_text: University: See College or University.

sources.title: Unmet Housing Needs Unit (UHNU)
sources.excerpt: Unmet Housing Needs Unit (UHNU): A dwelling unit for rent or for sale that is regulated pursuant to Section 9.02.
sources.full_text: Unmet Housing Needs Unit (UHNU): A dwelling unit for rent or for sale that is regulated pursuant to Section 9.02.

sources.title: Unmet Housing Needs Unit Program
sources.excerpt: Unmet Housing Needs Unit (UHNU) Program: The UHNU Program governs the provision and administration of UHNUs. The UHNU Program is implemented pursuant to Section 9.02, and applicable sections of Chapter 1450 of the Codified Ordinances of Loudoun County.
sources.full_text: Unmet Housing Needs Unit (UHNU) Program: The UHNU Program governs the provision and administration of UHNUs. The UHNU Program is implemented pursuant to Section 9.02, and applicable sections of Chapter 1450 of the Codified Ordinances of Loudoun County.

sources.title: Urban Deck
sources.excerpt: Urban Deck: A platform for landscaped greens or engineered to accommodate buildings, which spans over major roadways. The intent of an urban deck is to create and enable pedestrian movement across an otherwise, typically impenetrable barrier, and to provide space for activity that can link both sides of the roadway.
sources.full_text: Urban Deck: A platform for landscaped greens or engineered to accommodate buildings, which spans over major roadways. The intent of an urban deck is to create and enable pedestrian movement across an otherwise, typically impenetrable barrier, and to provide space for activity that can link both sides of the roadway.

sources.title: Urban Forester
sources.excerpt: Urban Forester: See Arborist or Urban Forester.
sources.full_text: Urban Forester: See Arborist or Urban Forester.

sources.title: Urban Growth Area
sources.excerpt: Urban Growth Area: Any area within the County that is currently served with public water and sewer or that is planned to be served with public water and sewer at sometime within the timeframe of the Comprehensive Plan.
sources.full_text: Urban Growth Area: Any area within the County that is currently served with public water and sewer or that is planned to be served with public water and sewer at sometime within the timeframe of the Comprehensive Plan.

sources.title: Use, Accessory
sources.excerpt: Use, Accessory: A use of a building, lot, or portion thereof, which is customarily incidental and subordinate to the principal use of the building or lot.
sources.full_text: Use, Accessory: A use of a building, lot, or portion thereof, which is customarily incidental and subordinate to the principal use of the building or lot.

sources.title: Use, Commercial
sources.excerpt: Use, Commercial: Any wholesale, retail, or service business activity established to carry on trade for a profit.
sources.full_text: Use, Commercial: Any wholesale, retail, or service business activity established to carry on trade for a profit.

sources.title: Use, Nonresidential
sources.excerpt: Use, Nonresidential: A principal use that is not listed under the Residential use classification of the use tables in Chapter 3 of this Zoning Ordinance.
sources.full_text: Use, Nonresidential: A principal use that is not listed under the Residential use classification of the use tables in Chapter 3 of this Zoning Ordinance.

sources.title: Use, Principal
sources.excerpt: Use, Principal: The primary use and chief purpose for which a lot or the principal building thereon is designed, arranged, or intended and for which it is or may be used, occupied, or maintained.
sources.full_text: Use, Principal: The primary use and chief purpose for which a lot or the principal building thereon is designed, arranged, or intended and for which it is or may be used, occupied, or maintained.

sources.title: Use, Residential
sources.excerpt: Use, Residential: A use that is listed under the Household Living and Group Living use categories in the Residential use classification of the use tables in Chapter 3 of this Zoning Ordinance.
sources.full_text: Use, Residential: A use that is listed under the Household Living and Group Living use categories in the Residential use classification of the use tables in Chapter 3 of this Zoning Ordinance.

sources.title: Use, Temporary
sources.excerpt: Use, Temporary: A use of land limited in both duration and the number of annual occurrences, excluding uses and events customarily associated with the principal land use (e.g., weddings at a church, sporting events at a stadium).
sources.full_text: Use, Temporary: A use of land limited in both duration and the number of annual occurrences, excluding uses and events customarily associated with the principal land use (e.g., weddings at a church, sporting events at a stadium).

sources.title: Utilities
sources.excerpt: Utilities: A use category composed of facilities for the provision of infrastructure services that support legally established uses and that need to be located in or near the area where the service is provided. Accessory uses may include control, monitoring, data, or transmission equipment.
sources.full_text: Utilities: A use category composed of facilities for the provision of infrastructure services that support legally established uses and that need to be located in or near the area where the service is provided. Accessory uses may include control, monitoring, data, or transmission equipment.

sources.title: Utility Line
sources.excerpt: Utility Line: A line suspended overhead on utility poles or buried underground that is used for the transmission and/or distribution, or conveyance, of public water, public sewer, natural gas, telephone, or electricity. Pursuant to Code of Virginia § 56-46.1., electrical transmission lines of 138 KV or more, approved by the State Corporation Commission, are deemed to have satisfied the requirements of the Zoning Ordinance.
sources.full_text: Utility Line: A line suspended overhead on utility poles or buried underground that is used for the transmission and/or distribution, or conveyance, of public water, public sewer, natural gas, telephone, or electricity. Pursuant to Code of Virginia § 56-46.1., electrical transmission lines of 138 KV or more, approved by the State Corporation Commission, are deemed to have satisfied the requirements of the Zoning Ordinance.

sources.title: Utility, Major
sources.excerpt: Utility, Major: Major facilities related to the provision, distribution, collection, transmission, or disposal of public water or public sewer, or telephone, cable, electricity, or natural gas owned and operated by a public utility (as defined under Section 56-232 of the Code of Virginia) or a public service corporation (as defined under Section 56-1 of the Code of Virginia), necessary to support legally established uses and that do not meet the definition of "Utility, Minor". Includes the facil
sources.full_text: Utility, Major: Major facilities related to the provision, distribution, collection, transmission, or disposal of public water or public sewer, or telephone, cable, electricity, or natural gas owned and operated by a public utility (as defined under Section 56-232 of the Code of Virginia) or a public service corporation (as defined under Section 56-1 of the Code of Virginia), necessary to support legally established uses and that do not meet the definition of "Utility, Minor". Includes the facilities listed below, and accessory storage areas for vehicles and equipment necessary to provide those services, and accessory structures such as switch boxes, transformer boxes, and cap banks. Does not include telecommunications. A. Water Supply Reservoir: A dam and impoundment area created and operated to store water for public water, including necessary or customary appurtenant facilities such as access roads and transmission pipes. Water storage tanks and water treatment plants are separate uses not included as part of a water supply reservoir. B. Sewage Treatment Plant: A plant for the primary, secondary, or tertiary treatment of sewage for public sewer. C. Utility Substation: A facility that is part of a regional interconnecting grid system or dedicated to an individual user for the transformation, transmission, switching of transmission and/or distribution voltages, and/or distribution of usable/consumable electric power; or transmission of natural gas, or television or telephone signals. D. Water Storage Tank: A tower or other facility for the storage of water for public water. E. Water Treatment Plant: A facility for the purification of potable water for public water.

sources.title: Utility, Minor
sources.excerpt: Utility, Minor: A minor facility or other structure for the provision, distribution, collection, transmission, or disposal of public water, public sewer, telephone, cable, electricity, and natural gas necessary to support legally established uses. Includes the facilities and structures listed below, and accessory structures such as switch boxes, transformer boxes, cap banks, and meters. Does not include telecommunications. Generally, does not have employees on site. For purposes of Natural and E
sources.full_text: Utility, Minor: A minor facility or other structure for the provision, distribution, collection, transmission, or disposal of public water, public sewer, telephone, cable, electricity, and natural gas necessary to support legally established uses. Includes the facilities and structures listed below, and accessory structures such as switch boxes, transformer boxes, cap banks, and meters. Does not include telecommunications. Generally, does not have employees on site. For purposes of Natural and Environmental Resources (NERS), may include flood control or stormwater drainage infrastructure. A. Sewer Pumping Station: A public sewer facility with electric-powered pumps, designed and constructed to raise wastewater in elevation, pump effluent uphill to a gravity feed sewer line, or overcome head losses due to pipeline friction. B. Water Pumping Station: A public water facility for the pumping of potable water. C. Municipal Water Well: A well used to collect water for a municipal water supply system. Accessory uses include access roads, utility lines, generators, and well houses.

sources.title: Utility Pole
sources.excerpt: Utility Pole: A ground-mounted self-supporting vertical structure made of fabricated metal, treated wood, or concrete used to elevate electrical and communication distribution and/or transmission lines and antennas, whose primary function is the support of wires, conductors, and associated apparatus used for the distribution and/or transmission of electrical energy and/or land line communication signals.
sources.full_text: Utility Pole: A ground-mounted self-supporting vertical structure made of fabricated metal, treated wood, or concrete used to elevate electrical and communication distribution and/or transmission lines and antennas, whose primary function is the support of wires, conductors, and associated apparatus used for the distribution and/or transmission of electrical energy and/or land line communication signals.

sources.title: Utility Substation
sources.excerpt: Utility Substation: See Utility, Major.
sources.full_text: Utility Substation: See Utility, Major.

sources.title: Vacant Land
sources.excerpt: Vacant Land: A lot or parcel of land on which no improvements have been constructed.
sources.full_text: Vacant Land: A lot or parcel of land on which no improvements have been constructed.

sources.title: Variable Riparian Preservation Buffer
sources.excerpt: Variable Riparian Preservation Buffer: River and Stream Corridor Resources (RSCR) buffer area that varies in width as needed to achieve a required minimum buffer width, beginning from and extending away from the Riparian Protection Buffer and associated water body, as provided in accordance with Table 6.01-1.
sources.full_text: Variable Riparian Preservation Buffer: River and Stream Corridor Resources (RSCR) buffer area that varies in width as needed to achieve a required minimum buffer width, beginning from and extending away from the Riparian Protection Buffer and associated water body, as provided in accordance with Table 6.01-1.

sources.title: Variance
sources.excerpt: Variance: A reasonable deviation from those provisions regulating the shape, size, or area of a lot or parcel of land or the size, height, area, bulk, or location of a building or structure when the strict application of the Zoning Ordinance would unreasonably restrict the utilization of the property, and such need for a variance would not be shared generally by other properties, and provided such variance is not contrary to the purpose of the Zoning Ordinance. Variances do not include a change
sources.full_text: Variance: A reasonable deviation from those provisions regulating the shape, size, or area of a lot or parcel of land or the size, height, area, bulk, or location of a building or structure when the strict application of the Zoning Ordinance would unreasonably restrict the utilization of the property, and such need for a variance would not be shared generally by other properties, and provided such variance is not contrary to the purpose of the Zoning Ordinance. Variances do not include a change in use. See Section 10.08.

sources.title: VDOT
sources.excerpt: VDOT: The Virginia Department of Transportation.
sources.full_text: VDOT: The Virginia Department of Transportation.

sources.title: VDOT Resident Engineer
sources.excerpt: VDOT Resident Engineer: The Resident Engineer for Loudoun County of the Virginia Department of Transportation, or his designated deputy.
sources.full_text: VDOT Resident Engineer: The Resident Engineer for Loudoun County of the Virginia Department of Transportation, or his designated deputy.

sources.title: Vegetative Waste
sources.excerpt: Vegetative Waste: The decomposable materials generated by yard and lawn care or land clearing activities, and includes, but is not limited to, leaves, grass trimmings, and woody waste such as shrub and tree prunings, bark, limbs, roots, and stumps.
sources.full_text: Vegetative Waste: The decomposable materials generated by yard and lawn care or land clearing activities, and includes, but is not limited to, leaves, grass trimmings, and woody waste such as shrub and tree prunings, bark, limbs, roots, and stumps.

sources.title: Vegetative Waste Management Facility
sources.excerpt: Vegetative Waste Management Facility: A solid waste management facility that manages vegetative waste (as defined by Code of Virginia § 10.1-1400).
sources.full_text: Vegetative Waste Management Facility: A solid waste management facility that manages vegetative waste (as defined by Code of Virginia § 10.1-1400).

sources.title: Vehicle, Business
sources.excerpt: Vehicle, Business: A vehicle associated with a business. Business vehicles must not exceed a rated capacity of 1 and 1/2 tons and must not have more than 2 axles.
sources.full_text: Vehicle, Business: A vehicle associated with a business. Business vehicles must not exceed a rated capacity of 1 and 1/2 tons and must not have more than 2 axles.

sources.title: Vehicle Charging Station
sources.excerpt: Vehicle Charging Station: A parking space that is served by equipment that transfers electric energy (by conductive or inductive means) to a battery or other energy storage device in an electric vehicle.
sources.full_text: Vehicle Charging Station: A parking space that is served by equipment that transfers electric energy (by conductive or inductive means) to a battery or other energy storage device in an electric vehicle.

sources.title: Vehicle Repair, Heavy
sources.excerpt: Vehicle Repair, Heavy: An establishment engaged in major mechanical and body work performed on vehicles, repair of transmissions and differentials, straightening of body parts, painting, welding, or similar work. Accessory uses include light vehicle repair establishments, but do not include establishments for the sale, rental, and service of heavy equipment and specialized vehicles.
sources.full_text: Vehicle Repair, Heavy: An establishment engaged in major mechanical and body work performed on vehicles, repair of transmissions and differentials, straightening of body parts, painting, welding, or similar work. Accessory uses include light vehicle repair establishments, but do not include establishments for the sale, rental, and service of heavy equipment and specialized vehicles.

sources.title: Vehicle Repair, Light
sources.excerpt: Vehicle Repair, Light: An establishment where the primary use is the sale, servicing, repair and/or installation of gas, electric, or hybrid motor vehicle accessories, such as: spark plugs, batteries, distributors and distributor parts, tires, brakes, brake fluid, mufflers, tail pipes, water hoses, fan belts, light bulbs, fuses, floor mats, windshield wipers, wiper blades, grease retainers, wheel bearings, and mirrors. Light vehicle repair may also include greasing, lubrication and radiator flus
sources.full_text: Vehicle Repair, Light: An establishment where the primary use is the sale, servicing, repair and/or installation of gas, electric, or hybrid motor vehicle accessories, such as: spark plugs, batteries, distributors and distributor parts, tires, brakes, brake fluid, mufflers, tail pipes, water hoses, fan belts, light bulbs, fuses, floor mats, windshield wipers, wiper blades, grease retainers, wheel bearings, and mirrors. Light vehicle repair may also include greasing, lubrication and radiator flushing, minor servicing and repair of carburetors, fuel pumps, oil pumps, water pumps and lines, electrical systems, and minor motor adjustments not involving removal of the head or crankcase or racing the motor.

sources.title: Vehicle Sales and/or Rental
sources.excerpt: Vehicle Sales and/or Rental: An establishment where the principal occupation is the sale and/or rental and accessory service of vehicles stored on-site and in operating condition. "Vehicles" include automobiles, motorcycles, All-Terrain Vehicles (ATV), and trucks stored on site. "Vehicles" do not include heavy equipment and specialized vehicle sale, rental, and service establishments. However, specialized vehicles may be sold, rented, and serviced as an accessory use.
sources.full_text: Vehicle Sales and/or Rental: An establishment where the principal occupation is the sale and/or rental and accessory service of vehicles stored on-site and in operating condition. "Vehicles" include automobiles, motorcycles, All-Terrain Vehicles (ATV), and trucks stored on site. "Vehicles" do not include heavy equipment and specialized vehicle sale, rental, and service establishments. However, specialized vehicles may be sold, rented, and serviced as an accessory use.

sources.title: Vehicle Service Station
sources.excerpt: Vehicle Service Station: An establishment where gasoline, petroleum, diesel fuel, oil, grease, batteries, tires, or automobile accessories are dispensed at retail as a principal use and/or where, in addition, only the following services are rendered and sales made: A. Sale and servicing of spark plugs, batteries, and distributor parts; B. Tire sales, servicing, and repair, but not recapping or regrooving; C. Replacement or adjustment of mufflers and tailpipes, water hoses, fan belts, brake fluid
sources.full_text: Vehicle Service Station: An establishment where gasoline, petroleum, diesel fuel, oil, grease, batteries, tires, or automobile accessories are dispensed at retail as a principal use and/or where, in addition, only the following services are rendered and sales made: A. Sale and servicing of spark plugs, batteries, and distributor parts; B. Tire sales, servicing, and repair, but not recapping or regrooving; C. Replacement or adjustment of mufflers and tailpipes, water hoses, fan belts, brake fluid, light bulbs, fuses, floor mats, seat covers, windshield wipers, and wiper blades, grease retainers, wheel bearings, mirrors, and the like; D. Radiator cleaning and flushing, provision of water, anti-freeze, and the like; E. Washing and polishing, and sale of automotive washing and polishing materials; F. Greasing and lubrication; G. Providing and repairing fuel pumps, oil pumps, and lines; H. Servicing and repair of carburetors; I. Emergency wiring repairs; J. Adjusting and repairing brakes; K. Minor motor adjustments not involving removal of the head or crankcase or racing the motor; L. Provision of convenience goods for gasoline supply station customers; M. Provision of road maps and other information material to customers; N. Provision of restroom facilities; O. Performing State vehicle inspections; P. Car wash; and/or Q. Electric vehicle charging spaces. A vehicle service station does not include major mechanical and body work, straightening of frames of body parts, steam cleaning, painting, welding, storage of automobiles not in operating condition, or any activity involving noise, glare, fumes, smoke, or other characteristics to an extent greater than normally found in vehicle service stations.

sources.title: Vehicle Storage and Impoundment
sources.excerpt: Vehicle Storage and Impoundment: An area designed for the temporary storage of wrecked, inoperable and/or abandoned motor vehicles. This does not include the dismantling, wrecking, or sale of motor vehicles or parts.
sources.full_text: Vehicle Storage and Impoundment: An area designed for the temporary storage of wrecked, inoperable and/or abandoned motor vehicles. This does not include the dismantling, wrecking, or sale of motor vehicles or parts.

sources.title: Vehicle Wholesale Auction
sources.excerpt: Vehicle Wholesale Auction: An establishment that sells or offers for sale motor vehicles, at auctions on a wholesale basis, only to licensed new or used vehicle dealers or wholesalers. Accessory uses may include services such as title processing, clean-up, and light service and repair of vehicles for sale for auction, the sale of food to customers, financial services, test-drive track, the storage or marshaling of auction vehicles, and the sale of specialty vehicles at auctions on a wholesale ba
sources.full_text: Vehicle Wholesale Auction: An establishment that sells or offers for sale motor vehicles, at auctions on a wholesale basis, only to licensed new or used vehicle dealers or wholesalers. Accessory uses may include services such as title processing, clean-up, and light service and repair of vehicles for sale for auction, the sale of food to customers, financial services, test-drive track, the storage or marshaling of auction vehicles, and the sale of specialty vehicles at auctions on a wholesale basis.

sources.title: Vehicular Access
sources.excerpt: Vehicular Access: Required access to any lot that must be provided at a width sufficient to provide necessary emergency access and as required by Section 4.300. Design and Construction Standards of the Facilities Standards Manual.
sources.full_text: Vehicular Access: Required access to any lot that must be provided at a width sufficient to provide necessary emergency access and as required by Section 4.300. Design and Construction Standards of the Facilities Standards Manual.

sources.title: Vertical Cost
sources.excerpt: Vertical Cost: The cost of constructing a prototypical single-family detached (SFD) affordable dwelling unit (ADU) as established on a semiannual basis by the Affordable Dwelling Unit Advisory Board (ADUAB). Vertical cost does not include the cost of land for the ADU.
sources.full_text: Vertical Cost: The cost of constructing a prototypical single-family detached (SFD) affordable dwelling unit (ADU) as established on a semiannual basis by the Affordable Dwelling Unit Advisory Board (ADUAB). Vertical cost does not include the cost of land for the ADU.

sources.title: Very Steep Slope Area
sources.excerpt: Very Steep Slope Area: Naturally occurring land areas with slopes greater than 25%. The Steep Slope Standards do not apply to slopes created by permitted land disturbing activities, such as, but not limited to, building and parking pad sites; berms; temporary construction stockpiles; and road-side ditches; also referred to as Adjacent Steep Slopes when located inside of River and Stream Corridor Resources.
sources.full_text: Very Steep Slope Area: Naturally occurring land areas with slopes greater than 25%. The Steep Slope Standards do not apply to slopes created by permitted land disturbing activities, such as, but not limited to, building and parking pad sites; berms; temporary construction stockpiles; and road-side ditches; also referred to as Adjacent Steep Slopes when located inside of River and Stream Corridor Resources.

sources.title: Veterinary Service
sources.excerpt: Veterinary Service: A service for the medical or custodial care of animals where the animals are not brought to the establishment but are cared for on an off-site basis. Accessory uses include an office and storage areas for equipment and supplies necessary for conducting the veterinary service. This use does not include crematory facilities.
sources.full_text: Veterinary Service: A service for the medical or custodial care of animals where the animals are not brought to the establishment but are cared for on an off-site basis. Accessory uses include an office and storage areas for equipment and supplies necessary for conducting the veterinary service. This use does not include crematory facilities.

sources.title: Village Green
sources.excerpt: Village Green: The primary public open space located within the Rural Village Residential Area that primarily contains a well-defined, generally permeable green space with landscaped areas, pedestrian ways and passive recreational areas, or any combination thereof. A mix of uses, including storefront, civic, and residential must front the Village Green.
sources.full_text: Village Green: The primary public open space located within the Rural Village Residential Area that primarily contains a well-defined, generally permeable green space with landscaped areas, pedestrian ways and passive recreational areas, or any combination thereof. A mix of uses, including storefront, civic, and residential must front the Village Green.

sources.title: Village Square
sources.excerpt: Village Square: An open, generally impervious, public space used for group gatherings, displays, meetings, concerts, or other community activities.
sources.full_text: Village Square: An open, generally impervious, public space used for group gatherings, displays, meetings, concerts, or other community activities.

sources.title: Virginia Landmarks Register
sources.excerpt: Virginia Landmarks Register: A listing of historic properties in the Commonwealth of Virginia as maintained by the Virginia Department of Historic Resources.
sources.full_text: Virginia Landmarks Register: A listing of historic properties in the Commonwealth of Virginia as maintained by the Virginia Department of Historic Resources.

sources.title: Visitor Accommodation
sources.excerpt: Visitor Accommodation: Uses composed of lodging units where tenancy may be arranged for periods of fewer than 30 days. Accessory uses include parking, laundry, recreational uses, and food services.
sources.full_text: Visitor Accommodation: Uses composed of lodging units where tenancy may be arranged for periods of fewer than 30 days. Accessory uses include parking, laundry, recreational uses, and food services.

sources.title: Warehousing Facility
sources.excerpt: Warehousing Facility: A building used primarily for the holding or storage of goods and merchandise. A mini-warehousing establishment is a separate and distinct use.
sources.full_text: Warehousing Facility: A building used primarily for the holding or storage of goods and merchandise. A mini-warehousing establishment is a separate and distinct use.

sources.title: Waste-Related Uses
sources.excerpt: Waste-Related Uses: Uses that collect sanitary waste or uses that manufacture or produce goods or energy from the composting of organic materials or processing of organic or related waste materials, or the collection or stockpiling of dirt. Waste-related uses do not include uses that receive hazardous wastes from others or that process other than organic material or related waste materials. Accessory uses include offices and repackaging and transshipment of by-products.
sources.full_text: Waste-Related Uses: Uses that collect sanitary waste or uses that manufacture or produce goods or energy from the composting of organic materials or processing of organic or related waste materials, or the collection or stockpiling of dirt. Waste-related uses do not include uses that receive hazardous wastes from others or that process other than organic material or related waste materials. Accessory uses include offices and repackaging and transshipment of by-products.

sources.title: Water Extraction
sources.excerpt: Water Extraction: The use of an on-site well, spring, or any other water source for the extraction or collection of water for off-site use. Water Extraction does not include Water Well, Municipal, or Rainwater Harvesting.
sources.full_text: Water Extraction: The use of an on-site well, spring, or any other water source for the extraction or collection of water for off-site use. Water Extraction does not include Water Well, Municipal, or Rainwater Harvesting.

sources.title: Water Management Plan
sources.excerpt: Water Management Plan: A plan of irrigation indicating a sustainable water usage rate, identifying the water source(s), establishing a schedule of withdrawal, and providing methods for mitigation of undesirable effects.
sources.full_text: Water Management Plan: A plan of irrigation indicating a sustainable water usage rate, identifying the water source(s), establishing a schedule of withdrawal, and providing methods for mitigation of undesirable effects.

sources.title: Water, Public
sources.excerpt: Water, Public: A central, community, or municipal water supply system for the purpose of furnishing potable water. A. Central Water Supply: The water supply system for eastern Loudoun County owned and operated by Loudoun Water. B. Water Supply System, Community: A water supply system owned or operated by Loudoun Water that is designed to serve small-scale development, including clusters, in conformance to Section 7.08. Such system may serve only 1 lot, where a community system is required by Cha
sources.full_text: Water, Public: A central, community, or municipal water supply system for the purpose of furnishing potable water. A. Central Water Supply: The water supply system for eastern Loudoun County owned and operated by Loudoun Water. B. Water Supply System, Community: A water supply system owned or operated by Loudoun Water that is designed to serve small-scale development, including clusters, in conformance to Section 7.08. Such system may serve only 1 lot, where a community system is required by Chapter 4 for a specific use. C. Water Supply System, Municipal: A water supply system that is owned or operated by 1 of the incorporated towns within Loudoun County.

sources.title: Water Pumping Station
sources.excerpt: Water Pumping Station: See Utility, Minor.
sources.full_text: Water Pumping Station: See Utility, Minor.

sources.title: Water Supply Reservoir
sources.excerpt: Water Supply Reservoir: See Utility, Major.
sources.full_text: Water Supply Reservoir: See Utility, Major.

sources.title: Water Supply System, Central
sources.excerpt: Water Supply System, Central: See Water, Public.
sources.full_text: Water Supply System, Central: See Water, Public.

sources.title: Water Supply System, Community
sources.excerpt: Water Supply System, Community: See Water, Public.
sources.full_text: Water Supply System, Community: See Water, Public.

sources.title: Water Supply System, Individual
sources.excerpt: Water Supply System, Individual: A private water supply system approved by the Loudoun County Health Department located on the lot served unless permitted to be located off-site under Section 7.08.
sources.full_text: Water Supply System, Individual: A private water supply system approved by the Loudoun County Health Department located on the lot served unless permitted to be located off-site under Section 7.08.

sources.title: Water Supply System, Municipal
sources.excerpt: Water Supply System, Municipal: See Water, Public.
sources.full_text: Water Supply System, Municipal: See Water, Public.

sources.title: Water Storage Tank
sources.excerpt: Water Storage Tank: See Utility, Major.
sources.full_text: Water Storage Tank: See Utility, Major.

sources.title: Water Treatment Plant
sources.excerpt: Water Pumping Station: See Utility, Major.
sources.full_text: Water Pumping Station: See Utility, Major.

sources.title: Water Well, Municipal
sources.excerpt: Water Well, Municipal: See Utility, Minor.
sources.full_text: Water Well, Municipal: See Utility, Minor.

sources.title: Wayside Stand
sources.excerpt: Wayside Stand: Any structure or land on a farm used by the property owner, their family, or tenants to sell agricultural or horticultural produce, livestock, or merchandise principally produced on that farm, which is clearly an accessory use of the premises and does not change its character. Produce grown on other farms and accessory products also may be sold.
sources.full_text: Wayside Stand: Any structure or land on a farm used by the property owner, their family, or tenants to sell agricultural or horticultural produce, livestock, or merchandise principally produced on that farm, which is clearly an accessory use of the premises and does not change its character. Produce grown on other farms and accessory products also may be sold.

sources.title: Wetland
sources.excerpt: Wetland: Those areas that are inundated or saturated by surface or groundwater at a frequency and duration sufficient to support, and that, under normal circumstances do support, a prevalence of vegetation typically adapted for life in saturated soil conditions, as identified by the United States Army Corps of Engineers or the Commonwealth of Virginia. Wetlands generally include swamps, marshes, bogs, and similar areas.
sources.full_text: Wetland: Those areas that are inundated or saturated by surface or groundwater at a frequency and duration sufficient to support, and that, under normal circumstances do support, a prevalence of vegetation typically adapted for life in saturated soil conditions, as identified by the United States Army Corps of Engineers or the Commonwealth of Virginia. Wetlands generally include swamps, marshes, bogs, and similar areas.

sources.title: Wetland Mitigation
sources.excerpt: Wetland Mitigation: Wetland enhancement, restoration, creation and/or preservation project that serves to offset unavoidable impacts to wetlands, wetland buffer areas, and other associated natural habitats. A Wetland Mitigation Bank is a type of Wetland Mitigation.
sources.full_text: Wetland Mitigation: Wetland enhancement, restoration, creation and/or preservation project that serves to offset unavoidable impacts to wetlands, wetland buffer areas, and other associated natural habitats. A Wetland Mitigation Bank is a type of Wetland Mitigation.

sources.title: Wetland Mitigation Bank
sources.excerpt: Wetland Mitigation Bank: A natural resource management technique authorized by Part 404 of the federal Clean Water Act, or other state or federal law, as applicable, using wetland preservation, restoration, creation and/or enhancement to offset or replace wetland functions that are lost due to development. Wetland mitigation banks are typically large areas of wetlands operated by private or public entities, which may sell credits to other entities to compensate for wetland loss or impact at deve
sources.full_text: Wetland Mitigation Bank: A natural resource management technique authorized by Part 404 of the federal Clean Water Act, or other state or federal law, as applicable, using wetland preservation, restoration, creation and/or enhancement to offset or replace wetland functions that are lost due to development. Wetland mitigation banks are typically large areas of wetlands operated by private or public entities, which may sell credits to other entities to compensate for wetland loss or impact at development sites or enter into other similar arrangements.

sources.title: Wholesale Distribution, Warehousing and Storage
sources.excerpt: Wholesale Distribution, Warehousing, and Storage: The storage or holding of goods, which may include the sale of goods to other firms for resale, including activities involving significant storage and movement of products or equipment. This use does not involve manufacturing or production. Examples include: A. Carting; B. Cold storage; C. Distribution facilities (as defined below); D. Apparel wholesale; E. Express crating; F. Hauling; G. Feed locker plants; H. Fulfillment centers that combine st
sources.full_text: Wholesale Distribution, Warehousing, and Storage: The storage or holding of goods, which may include the sale of goods to other firms for resale, including activities involving significant storage and movement of products or equipment. This use does not involve manufacturing or production. Examples include: A. Carting; B. Cold storage; C. Distribution facilities (as defined below); D. Apparel wholesale; E. Express crating; F. Hauling; G. Feed locker plants; H. Fulfillment centers that combine storage with call centers; I. Hardware storage; J. Merchant wholesalers (such as restaurant supply sales); K. Warehousing of materials; L. Wholesale sale of paper supplies, shoes, sporting goods, professional and commercial equipment and supplies; and M. Otherwise preparing goods for transportation. A "Distribution Facility" means the intake of goods and merchandise, individually or in bulk, the short-term holding or storage of such goods or merchandise, and/or the breaking up into lots or parcels and subsequent shipment off-site of such goods and merchandise. Distribution may be provided to an entity with an identity of interest with the distribution facility or to businesses and individuals unrelated to the distributor. The term "Distribution Facility" also includes a transshipment facility for the temporary holding, storage, and shipment of goods or vehicles.

sources.title: Winery, Commercial
sources.excerpt: Winery, Commercial: An establishment with facilities for making and bottling wine for sale on site or through wholesale or retail outlets. A commercial winery is a winery that does not meet the definition of a Farm Winery.
sources.full_text: Winery, Commercial: An establishment with facilities for making and bottling wine for sale on site or through wholesale or retail outlets. A commercial winery is a winery that does not meet the definition of a Farm Winery.

sources.title: Winery, Farm
sources.excerpt: Winery, Farm: An establishment: (i) located on a farm with a producing vineyard, orchard or similar growing area and with facilities for fermenting and bottling wine on the premises where the owner or lessee manufactures wine that contains not more than 18% alcohol by volume; or (ii) located in the Commonwealth with a producing vineyard, orchard or similar growing area or agreements for purchasing grapes or fruits from agricultural growers within the Commonwealth, and with facilities for ferment
sources.full_text: Winery, Farm: An establishment: (i) located on a farm with a producing vineyard, orchard or similar growing area and with facilities for fermenting and bottling wine on the premises where the owner or lessee manufactures wine that contains not more than 18% alcohol by volume; or (ii) located in the Commonwealth with a producing vineyard, orchard or similar growing area or agreements for purchasing grapes or fruits from agricultural growers within the Commonwealth, and with facilities for fermenting and bottling wine on the premises where the owner or lessee manufactures wine that contains not more than 18% alcohol by volume. As used in this definition, the term owner or lessee must include a cooperative formed by an association of individuals for the purposes of manufacturing wine.
